### How the field sees the world

Human-computer interaction embeds a worldview that is substantially distinctive in its substantial humanism among CS subjects.

The first commitment is *to taking substantial users seriously*. The HCI worldview holds that substantial design must take substantial users seriously as substantial intelligent agents with substantial purposes, substantial constraints, and substantial dignity, rather than as obstacles to engineering or as objects to be manipulated for substantial business metrics. This commitment runs against engineering dispositions that treat user concerns as secondary to technical concerns. The HCI practitioner takes a different view: substantial systems exist to serve substantial users, and substantial design must center substantial user concerns as integral rather than auxiliary. The disposition has substantial consequences for how substantial design is approached and produces substantial work substantially different from alternatives.

The second commitment is *to substantial empirical evaluation of substantial designs*. The HCI worldview holds that substantial design intuitions are unreliable and that substantial empirical evaluation is integral to substantial design rather than auxiliary. This commitment shapes substantial practice: with substantial usability testing as routine practice, with substantial appreciation for what evaluation reveals, with substantial humility about substantial design judgment that has not been validated empirically. Practitioners who have absorbed this commitment treat empirical evaluation as integral engineering practice rather than as nice-to-have addition.

The third commitment is *to substantial design for substantial diversity*. The HCI worldview takes seriously that substantial users are substantially diverse — in substantial abilities, substantial cultural backgrounds, substantial technical fluency, the substantial various dimensions of variation — and that substantial design must accommodate this substantial diversity. This commitment runs against engineering dispositions that design for substantial average users or for substantial users similar to designers. Practitioners who have absorbed this commitment design with substantial attention to substantial diversity, treat substantial accessibility as integral, and recognize that designs serving narrow user contexts substantially fail substantial broader populations.

The fourth commitment is *to substantial integration with substantial cognitive science*. The HCI worldview takes seriously that substantial scientific understanding of human cognition, perception, and behavior provides substantial foundation for substantial design, and that substantial design without this scientific grounding is substantially less reliable than substantial design with it. This commitment runs against substantial design dispositions that treat substantial design as exclusively artistic concern. Practitioners who have absorbed it engage substantially with substantial cognitive science literature and integrate scientific understanding into substantial design practice.

The fifth commitment is *to substantial humility about substantial design judgment*. The HCI worldview takes seriously that substantial design intuitions are unreliable, that substantial documented evaluation routinely contradicts substantial designer expectations, and that substantial humility about substantial design judgment is integral to substantial competent practice. This commitment runs against substantial designer dispositions that treat substantial design judgment as substantial professional expertise sufficient on its own. Practitioners who have absorbed it treat substantial design claims with substantial care, validate substantial designs through substantial evaluation, and recognize that substantial designer experience is necessary but insufficient.

The sixth commitment is *to substantial cross-disciplinary engagement*. The HCI worldview takes substantial cross-disciplinary engagement seriously — with substantial psychology, with substantial design fields, with substantial sociology and anthropology, with substantial communication studies, the substantial various other disciplines. This commitment runs against substantial CS dispositions that prefer to remain within computer science. Practitioners who have absorbed this commitment engage substantially with adjacent disciplines as integral to substantial HCI practice, and recognize that substantial HCI is substantially interdisciplinary in ways that other CS subjects are not.

The seventh commitment is *to substantial integration of substantial design with substantial engineering and substantial broader concerns*. The HCI worldview holds that substantial design must integrate productively with substantial engineering and substantial broader product development concerns. This commitment runs against design dispositions that treat substantial design as concern separate from substantial engineering. Practitioners who have absorbed this commitment engage substantially with substantial engineering teams, contribute productively to substantial cross-functional development, and recognize that substantial design effectiveness depends substantially on substantial integration.

The eighth commitment is *to substantial responsibility for substantial broader implications*. The HCI worldview takes seriously that substantial design has substantial implications beyond immediate user experience — substantial implications for substantial users' attention, substantial implications for substantial broader social and cultural patterns, the substantial various other implications. This commitment runs against substantial design dispositions that focus narrowly on substantial immediate metrics. Practitioners who have absorbed this commitment engage with substantial broader implications, design with substantial awareness of substantial consequences beyond immediate use, and contribute to substantial design ethics rather than only to substantial design effectiveness in narrow senses.

These eight commitments — to taking users seriously, to empirical evaluation, to designing for diversity, to integration with cognitive science, to humility about design judgment, to cross-disciplinary engagement, to integration of design with engineering, and to responsibility for broader implications — distinguish the HCI worldview from adjacent dispositions. Practitioners who have absorbed them work in HCI as substantial intellectual and design practice rather than as application of UI conventions.

### Common pitfalls in learning it

The pitfalls of learning human-computer interaction are characteristic and substantially distinctive — the substantial integration of design and engineering, the substantial empirical character, and the substantial cross-disciplinary nature produce many opportunities for misdirected learning.

The most common pitfall is *the design-as-decoration misconception*. Substantial portions of public discussion of UI design treat the work as essentially aesthetic — choosing colors, choosing fonts, ensuring visual prettiness. This account substantially misrepresents substantial design work. The substantial intellectual content of HCI substantially extends beyond visual aesthetics to substantial concerns about substantial cognition, substantial behavior, substantial accessibility, the substantial various others. The countermeasure is to engage with substantial substantive HCI literature: to study substantial cognitive psychology foundations, to engage with substantial usability research, to recognize that substantial visual design is substantial part of HCI but not the whole.

A second pitfall is *the designer-as-user misconception*. Designers are substantially unrepresentative of substantial users — substantially more technically fluent, substantially more familiar with substantial design conventions, substantially differently positioned cognitively in substantial ways. Designers who substantially design for themselves produce substantial work that fails substantial broader user populations. The countermeasure is substantial engagement with substantial users different from designers: substantial user research, substantial usability testing with substantial actual users, substantial humility about substantial designer perspective.

A third pitfall is *the methodology-skipping problem*. Substantial HCI depends on substantial empirical methodology, and learners can come to skip methodology — designing without substantial user research, evaluating without substantial methodological rigor, drawing conclusions from substantial inadequate evidence. The result is substantial design work that lacks substantial empirical grounding and that substantially fails substantial users. The countermeasure is substantial engagement with substantial methodology: substantial study of substantial research methods, substantial practice with substantial evaluation methods, substantial appreciation for what substantial methodology reveals that substantial intuition cannot.

A fourth pitfall is *the accessibility-as-addon misconception*. Substantial accessibility is substantively integral to substantial HCI rather than substantial addon, but learners can come to treat accessibility as substantial extra concern to be addressed after substantial main design work is complete. The result is substantial designs that are substantially difficult to make accessible, substantial accessibility added late with substantial gaps, substantial users with substantial disabilities substantially failed by the design. The countermeasure is to integrate accessibility from substantial start of substantial design work: substantial study of substantial WCAG and substantial broader accessibility literature, substantial practice with substantial accessibility evaluation tools, substantial engagement with substantial users with substantial disabilities.

A fifth pitfall is *the trends-driven design problem*. Substantial design has substantial trends and substantial fashions that influence substantial practice, and learners can come to follow trends without substantial understanding of why specific approaches work or fail. The result is substantial designs that follow trends but that fail substantial users for substantial fundamental reasons. The countermeasure is to engage with substantial timeless design principles alongside substantial contemporary trends: to study substantial Norman, substantial Tognazzini, substantial Nielsen, the substantial classic literature, alongside substantial contemporary developments.

A sixth pitfall is *the engineer-design-divide*. Substantial development can suffer from substantial divide between substantial designers and substantial engineers, with substantial design and substantial engineering proceeding without substantial integration. Learners can come to treat this divide as natural and produce substantial work that fits this dysfunctional pattern. The countermeasure is to engage with substantial integration of substantial design and substantial engineering: substantial appreciation for substantial engineering constraints in substantial design, substantial appreciation for substantial design concerns in substantial engineering, substantial work on substantial collaboration patterns that produce substantial integration.

A seventh pitfall is *the metrics-myopia problem*. Substantial design can be evaluated by substantial metrics (substantial conversion rates, substantial engagement metrics, the substantial various others), and learners can come to optimize for substantial metrics without substantial appreciation for what they actually measure. The substantial result is substantial designs that perform well on metrics but that substantially fail substantial users in substantial ways the metrics do not capture. The countermeasure is to engage with substantial metric limitations: substantial appreciation for what specific metrics measure and what they miss, substantial use of substantial qualitative methods alongside substantial metrics, substantial humility about substantial metric-driven optimization.

An eighth pitfall is *the dark-pattern adoption problem*. Substantial design can produce substantial dark patterns — substantial designs that exploit substantial cognitive biases or substantial user limitations to substantial business benefit at substantial user cost. Learners can come to adopt these patterns without substantial recognition of their substantial ethical implications. The countermeasure is substantial engagement with substantial design ethics: substantial Brignull's documentation of substantial dark patterns, substantial various ethical design literature, substantial appreciation that substantial design has substantial ethical dimensions and that substantial designers have substantial responsibility for substantial design choices.

A ninth pitfall is *the narrow-context specialization without substantial breadth*. Substantial HCI has substantial diversity of substantial application contexts (substantial mobile, substantial desktop, substantial voice, substantial AR/VR, the substantial various others), and learners can come to specialize narrowly in one context while neglecting substantial conceptual content other contexts contribute. The countermeasure is to engage with substantial breadth of substantial HCI contexts, recognizing that substantial transferable lessons emerge from substantial cross-context engagement.

A tenth pitfall is *the LLM-shortcut problem* as applied to HCI. Modern AI tools can produce substantial design code, substantial wireframes, substantial design suggestions. The cost in HCI is substantial because substantial competent practice depends substantially on substantial empirical understanding of substantial users that develops through substantial direct engagement, on substantial humility about substantial design intuitions that develops through substantial documented failures, on substantial design judgment that develops through substantial practice. The countermeasure is to use AI tools as supplements to substantial direct engagement with substantial HCI work rather than as replacements for it.

An eleventh pitfall is *the resistance to substantial methodological rigor problem*. Substantial design fields have substantial tradition that sometimes resists substantial empirical rigor as foreign to substantial design thinking. Learners can come to adopt this resistance, with substantial result that substantial design work suffers from substantial empirical inadequacy. The countermeasure is substantial appreciation for substantial empirical methods as integral to substantial design rather than as substantial intrusion: substantial recognition that substantial design without substantial empirical grounding is substantially less reliable than substantial design with it, substantial appreciation that substantial methodological rigor and substantial design creativity are substantially compatible.

### The literature of the subject

The literature on human-computer interaction has substantial diverse character. The discipline draws substantially from substantial computer science, substantial psychology, substantial design fields, substantial social sciences, with substantial corresponding diversity of substantial literature. #### Canonical entry points

The substantial canonical introduction to HCI as discipline is Norman's *The Design of Everyday Things* (revised and expanded edition, 2013). The book is unusually accessible while substantially substantive, articulating substantial fundamental design principles with substantial engaging examples drawn from substantial range of substantial designed objects. The book has substantial canonical status across substantial design and substantial HCI fields, and substantial reading of it is essentially required for substantial HCI engagement. Norman's substantial subsequent books extend his substantial work.

For more academic HCI introduction, Rogers, Sharp, and Preece's *Interaction Design: Beyond Human-Computer Interaction* (sixth edition, 2023) is substantial. The book substantially covers substantial breadth of HCI with substantial appropriate depth for foundational study. Dix, Finlay, Abowd, and Beale's *Human-Computer Interaction* (third edition, 2003, free) covers similar material with somewhat older perspective.

For substantial usability engineering specifically, Nielsen's *Usability Engineering* (1993) is foundational despite its age, articulating substantial usability methodology that remains substantively valuable. Krug's *Don't Make Me Think* (third edition, 2014) provides substantial accessible introduction to substantial usability concepts. Tognazzini's various writings on substantial design principles (substantial Tog Magazine and the substantial various others) are substantially valuable.

For visual and interaction design specifically, Shneiderman, Plaisant, Cohen, Jacobs, Elmqvist, and Diakopoulos's *Designing the User Interface* (sixth edition, 2017) is substantial. Cooper, Reimann, Cronin, and Noessel's *About Face: The Essentials of Interaction Design* (fourth edition, 2014) substantially covers substantial interaction design principles. Tidwell, Brewer, and Valencia's *Designing Interfaces* (third edition, 2020) covers substantial interaction patterns with substantial breadth.

For substantial information architecture, Rosenfeld, Morville, and Arango's *Information Architecture for the Web and Beyond* (fourth edition, 2015) is substantial. Wodtke and Govella's *Information Architecture: Blueprints for the Web* covers similar material more accessibly.

For accessibility specifically, the substantial WCAG documentation (free) is authoritative reference. Horton and Quesenbery's *A Web for Everyone* and the substantial various accessibility books provide substantial introduction. The substantial Inclusive Components by Pickering covers substantial component-level accessibility.

For visual design foundations, Lupton's *Thinking with Type* covers substantial typography. Tufte's *The Visual Display of Quantitative Information* (mentioned in Section 6.2) is substantial for information visualization. Bringhurst's *The Elements of Typographic Style* is substantial typography reference.

For cognitive psychology foundations relevant to HCI, Anderson's *Cognitive Psychology and Its Implications* covers substantial foundations. Card, Moran, and Newell's *The Psychology of Human-Computer Interaction* is foundational despite its age.

For substantial mobile design specifically, Hoober and Berkman's *Designing Mobile Interfaces* and the substantial various Apple Human Interface Guidelines and Google Material Design documentation provide substantial reference.

The reading-order recommendation: Norman as foundational orienting text, Rogers, Sharp, and Preece for substantial academic breadth, Krug for substantial accessible usability introduction, the substantial WCAG material for accessibility, with substantial various specialty references for substantial specific subareas of interest. The substantial substantial Apple Human Interface Guidelines and Google Material Design documentation provide substantial platform-specific reference.

The various courses from major universities (Stanford CS147, Carnegie Mellon's substantial HCI program, the substantial various others) provide substantial pedagogical material. The substantial Interaction Design Foundation and substantial various online education platforms provide substantial structured curriculum.

*AI-mediated interaction at depth.* The substantial recent literature on AI-mediated interaction is rapidly developing. *Information architecture at depth.* Beyond Rosenfeld, Morville, and Arango, Wurman's substantial work, the substantial various IA books, the substantial information architecture community publications constitute substantial deeper material.

#### Heterodox correctives

Several positions are heterodox correctives within HCI and worth engagement for the perspective they provide.

*The substantial critical perspective on substantial mainstream UX.* Substantial critical writing questions whether substantial mainstream UX practice substantially serves substantial users or substantially serves substantial business interests at substantial user cost. The substantial various papers and writings on substantial dark patterns, on substantial deceptive design, on substantial attention economy critiques constitute substantial heterodox literature with substantial intellectual content.

*The substantial slow-design and humane-design perspective.* Substantial work argues that substantial mainstream contemporary design optimizes for substantial engagement metrics in substantial ways that substantially harm substantial users, and that substantial alternative design philosophies (substantial slow design, substantial humane technology, the substantial various others) would substantially better serve substantial users. The substantial Center for Humane Technology and the substantial various humane design writings constitute substantial heterodox literature.

*The substantial anti-design-thinking perspective.* The substantial design thinking methodology has been substantially fashionable in substantial business contexts, and substantial critical writing questions whether design thinking substantially produces substantial value or substantially serves as substantial corporate ritual. The substantial various critical writings on design thinking constitute substantial heterodox literature.

*The substantial accessibility-first perspective.* Various practitioners argue that substantial accessibility should be substantial design starting point rather than substantial concern integrated with substantial broader design, with substantial argument that substantial accessibility-first produces substantially better designs for substantial broader populations. The substantial inclusive design literature articulates this position.

*The substantial textual interface perspective.* Substantial work argues that substantial mainstream graphical user interface paradigm has substantial limitations and that substantial textual and command-line interfaces substantially better serve substantial expert users. The substantial Unix command-line tradition and substantial various critiques of GUI dominance constitute substantial heterodox literature.

*The substantial design-as-political-act perspective.* Substantial work positions substantial design as fundamentally political, with substantial designers having substantial responsibility for substantial broader implications of substantial design choices. Costanza-Chock's *Design Justice* and substantial subsequent work in this tradition constitute substantial heterodox literature with substantial intellectual content.

*The substantial radical disability studies perspective.* Substantial work questions whether substantial mainstream accessibility approaches substantially serve substantial people with disabilities or substantially serve substantial others' comfort with substantial accessibility while substantially failing substantial disabled users. The substantial various disability studies writings constitute substantial heterodox literature.

*The substantial AI-skeptical perspective on AI-mediated interaction.* Substantial work questions whether substantial AI integration into substantial interaction substantially serves substantial users or substantially serves substantial AI deployment incentives. The substantial various critical writings on substantial AI assistants and substantial chatbots constitute substantial heterodox literature.

#### What to skip and why

Books on UI design published before approximately 2010 should be approached with caution as primary references for contemporary practice, with substantial exceptions of foundational books (substantial Norman, substantial Card-Moran-Newell, the substantial various others that retain substantive value despite age).

Most marketing-driven books on specific UX methodologies should be approached as substantial practical references rather than as substantial foundational learning. The genre exists and is substantially uneven.

Books on design that present specific aesthetic styles as universally appropriate should be supplemented with substantial perspective on style trade-offs. Substantial styles serve substantial purposes but no style is universally appropriate.

Books on UX that abstract over substantial empirical methodology should be approached with substantial caution. Substantial UX substantially requires substantial empirical engagement.

Most substantial certification preparation books in UX should be approached as substantial certification preparation rather than as substantial substantive learning.

Most online UX tutorials are uneven in quality. The exceptions — the substantial Nielsen Norman Group articles, the substantial Smashing Magazine UX content, the substantial well-maintained materials from substantial design organizations — are valuable. The typical tutorial covers substantial introductory mechanics without substantial depth.

Books on AI-mediated interaction that predate substantial foundation models are substantially dated for contemporary practice.

Books on mobile design from substantial early mobile era (pre-2015) are substantially dated.

### A note on contemporary relevance

Human-computer interaction is among the more contemporarily active subjects on the application axis, with substantial developments substantially reshaping the discipline.

The most consequential contemporary development is the substantial AI-mediated interaction revolution. Substantial conversational interfaces, substantial AI-augmented applications, substantial AI assistants, the substantial various AI-mediated experiences have substantially changed substantial portions of substantial contemporary interaction. The substantial implications continue to develop, with substantial uncertainty about substantial design conventions for substantial AI-mediated interaction. The trajectory suggests continued substantial AI-HCI integration with substantial implications for substantial portions of HCI practice.

The second major development is the substantial maturation of substantial accessibility as substantial mainstream concern. The substantial integration of substantial accessibility into substantial mainstream design practice (rather than treating it as substantial specialty concern) has substantially advanced. The substantial regulatory developments (substantial European Accessibility Act, the substantial various other regulatory developments) have substantially elevated substantial accessibility requirements.

The third development is the substantial growth of substantial design systems as substantial mainstream practice. Substantial design systems have substantially become substantial standard infrastructure for substantial portions of substantial interface development, with substantial implications for how substantial design and substantial engineering integrate.

The fourth development is the substantial maturation of substantial mobile design as substantial primary context for substantial portions of users. Substantial mobile-first thinking has substantially become substantial mainstream, with substantial implications for substantial how interfaces are designed.

The fifth development is the substantial growth of substantial AR/VR and substantial spatial computing. The substantial Apple Vision Pro launch, substantial Meta Quest market growth, substantial various other developments have substantially elevated substantial spatial computing as substantial design context, though substantial trajectory remains uncertain.

The sixth development is the substantial growing concern about substantial attention economy and substantial dark patterns. The substantial documented harms of substantial attention-extracting design have produced substantial counter-movements toward substantial humane design, substantial slow design, the substantial various ethical alternatives.

The seventh development is the substantial growth of substantial inclusive and equity-oriented design as substantial substantive concerns. Substantial design justice movements, substantial inclusive design movements, the substantial various movements toward substantial broader user concerns have substantially developed.

The eighth development is the substantial growing engagement with substantial design ethics. Substantial recognition that substantial design has substantial ethical dimensions has substantially developed, with substantial implications for what substantial competent practice involves.

The ninth development is the substantial reckoning with substantial limitations of substantial existing UX research methodology. Substantial concerns about substantial sample diversity in substantial usability testing, substantial limitations of substantial controlled experiments, substantial difficulties of evaluating substantial AI-mediated interaction have produced substantial methodological reflection.

Despite these developments, the foundational character of HCI is substantially robust. The core concerns — designing for human cognition, designing for diversity, evaluating designs empirically, interaction across contexts, integration with broader concerns — remain as articulated decades ago. The contemporary developments add to this foundation rather than replacing it.

A final observation specific to HCI: among the application chapters, this is the one with the most substantial integration with substantial non-CS disciplines. Substantial competent practice substantially requires substantial engagement with substantial cognitive psychology, substantial design fields, substantial social sciences, the substantial various other disciplines. The substantial cross-disciplinary character is integral to substantial HCI competence, and practitioners pursuing substantial substantive HCI competence should expect substantial cross-disciplinary engagement substantively.

A second observation specific to this subject: HCI has substantial unique character among CS subjects through substantial integration of substantial design and substantial engineering, substantial integration of substantial empirical research and substantial creative work. The substantial integration produces substantial intellectual richness but also substantial difficulty — substantial competent practice substantially requires substantial fluency across substantial different modes of work, and substantial portions of substantial development organizations struggle with substantial integration. Practitioners pursuing substantial HCI competence should engage substantially with this integration as substantial central concern.

A third observation specific to this subject: HCI has substantial responsibility dimensions that substantial other CS subjects substantially share less directly. Substantial design substantially shapes substantial user experience and substantial broader social implications. Substantial competent practitioners take substantial responsibility for substantial design decisions seriously, recognizing that substantial design has substantial consequences for substantial users and substantial broader society that substantial designers cannot reasonably abdicate.

## 6.5 — Robotics

### What it is, properly

Robotics is the discipline concerned with computational systems that perceive their environments and take physical actions in those environments — substantial integration of substantial perception (sensing the world), substantial computation (processing sensed information and deciding what to do), and substantial actuation (acting physically on the world) in ways that produce substantial autonomous or semi-autonomous behavior. The subject treats embodied computation as substantial intellectual and engineering work, with characteristic mathematical content (substantial work on kinematics and dynamics, substantial control theory, substantial probabilistic robotics, substantial estimation theory), characteristic engineering content (substantial systems engineering across hardware and software, substantial work on real-time systems, substantial work on the substantial integration of substantial subsystems), characteristic application domains (industrial robotics, mobile robotics, manipulation, autonomous vehicles, surgical robotics, the substantial various others), and substantial intersection with substantial machine learning (substantial work on learning-based robotics, substantial deep reinforcement learning for robotics, substantial recent foundation-model-augmented robotics). The discipline has substantial historical depth, with substantial development from the 1960s onward through substantial industrial robotics, substantial subsequent development through substantial mobile robotics and substantial autonomous systems, and substantial contemporary development driven substantially by substantial machine learning advances and by substantial maturation of substantial sensors and substantial computational hardware.

The conventional account treats robotics as either substantial industrial automation (the substantial robot arms doing substantial repetitive tasks in substantial factories) or substantial future-AI fantasy (the substantial humanoid robots of substantial science fiction). Both accounts substantially misrepresent contemporary robotics. The conceptual core is the recognition that *embodied computation faces substantial challenges that disembodied computation does not*, that *substantial physical interaction with substantial uncertain environments produces substantial difficulties throughout the engineering*, that *substantial integration across substantial subsystems is integral rather than auxiliary*, and that *substantial robotics combines substantial mathematical, engineering, and increasingly machine learning concerns in substantially distinctive ways*. These recognitions distinguish substantial robotics engineering from the surface accounts that introductory materials sometimes provide.

A more honest account distinguishes several distinct concerns within the broader subject. *Kinematics and dynamics* is the substantial subdiscipline concerned with the substantial mathematics of how robots move — substantial work on forward and inverse kinematics, substantial work on Jacobians and singularities, substantial work on dynamics (how forces and torques produce motion), substantial work on the various coordinate frames and transformations that robotic computation depends on. *Control* is the substantial subdiscipline concerned with making robots execute desired motions — substantial classical control (PID and the substantial various others), substantial modern control (state-space methods, optimal control, the substantial various others), substantial robust control for handling substantial uncertainty, substantial adaptive control, the substantial work on contemporary substantial learning-based control. *Perception* is the substantial subdiscipline concerned with making sense of substantial sensor data — substantial computer vision for visual perception, substantial work on lidar and substantial other 3D sensing, substantial sensor fusion across substantial multiple sensor modalities, substantial work on object recognition, scene understanding, and the substantial various other perception tasks. *State estimation and SLAM* covers the substantial work on estimating substantial robot state and substantial environment state from substantial sensor data — substantial Kalman filtering and the substantial various extensions, substantial particle filters, substantial simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM), substantial visual-inertial odometry, the substantial various other estimation techniques. *Planning* is the substantial subdiscipline concerned with deciding what actions to take — substantial motion planning (finding paths through configuration spaces), substantial task planning (deciding what high-level actions to perform), substantial trajectory optimization, substantial work on planning under uncertainty (POMDPs and substantial various approaches), substantial work on multi-robot planning. *Manipulation* covers the substantial work on robots manipulating objects — substantial work on grasping, substantial work on contact-rich manipulation, substantial work on compliant manipulation, the substantial various other manipulation concerns. *Mobile robotics* covers substantial concerns specific to robots that move through environments — substantial wheeled robots, substantial legged robots, substantial flying robots (drones, the various others), substantial underwater robots. *Human-robot interaction* covers substantial work on robots interacting with humans — substantial collaborative robots in industrial contexts, substantial assistive robotics, substantial social robotics, substantial concerns about substantial human-robot communication. *Robot learning* covers the substantial work on robots that learn from experience — substantial learning from demonstration, substantial reinforcement learning for robotics, substantial sim-to-real transfer, the substantial recent work on substantial foundation-model-augmented robotics. *Multi-robot systems* covers substantial concerns of substantial systems with substantial multiple coordinating robots. *Robot software architectures* covers substantial systems engineering of substantial robotic software — substantial ROS (Robot Operating System), substantial real-time concerns, substantial work on substantial system-level architectures for robotics. *Specific application domains* (industrial robotics, autonomous vehicles, surgical robotics, agricultural robotics, the substantial various others) each have substantial specific concerns alongside the broader principles.

The intellectual content of robotics is distinctive in several ways. The mathematical foundation is substantial — substantial portions of robotics depend on substantial linear algebra, substantial probability theory, substantial optimization, substantial control theory, and engagement at depth requires substantial mathematical preparation. The integration character is substantial — substantial robotics integrates substantial concerns from substantial across computer science with substantial concerns from substantial mechanical engineering, substantial electrical engineering, substantial control engineering. The cyber-physical character is substantial — robots are substantial physical systems with substantial implications throughout the engineering. The substantial gap between substantial simulation and substantial reality is unusual — substantial portions of substantial robotics development happen in substantial simulation, but substantial deployment in substantial reality routinely reveals substantial issues that substantial simulation did not capture, with substantial implications for substantial methodology. The substantial cycles of substantial enthusiasm and substantial disappointment have been substantial through robotics history — substantial promises of substantial general-purpose robots have been substantially repeatedly made and substantially repeatedly unfulfilled, with substantial implications for how substantial contemporary claims should be evaluated. The contemporary integration with substantial machine learning is substantial — substantial recent developments have substantially reshaped substantial portions of robotics practice, particularly through substantial foundation models for robotics that have substantially emerged.

The reason this account matters is that robotics has become substantially central to substantial portions of substantial contemporary computing while remaining substantively challenging. The substantial deployment of substantial robotic systems in substantial consequential contexts (industrial automation, autonomous vehicles, the substantial various others) produces substantial demand for substantial competent robotics engineering. The substantial gap between substantial robotics enthusiasm in substantial popular discussion and substantial technical realities of robotics engineering produces substantial confusion that substantial competent practitioners must substantially navigate. The discipline of robotics as substantial serious engineering practice differs substantially from substantial robotics fantasies, and competent work requires substantial preparation that substantial popular accounts often understate.

### The deep problems the field tries to answer

Five problems organize robotics as a discipline.

**The problem of operating with substantial uncertainty about the world.** Robots operate in substantial environments that they perceive imperfectly — substantial sensors are noisy, substantial environments contain substantial unmodeled features, substantial actions have substantial unintended consequences, substantial dynamics are substantially uncertain. The substantial difficulty has substantial implications throughout robotics. The discipline includes substantial work on this problem: the substantial probabilistic robotics tradition (Thrun, Burgard, and Fox's substantial *Probabilistic Robotics* and the substantial subsequent literature), substantial work on Bayesian estimation, substantial work on planning under uncertainty (POMDPs and the substantial various approximations), substantial work on robust control, substantial work on sensor fusion. The intellectual content includes substantial mathematical content (substantial probability theory, substantial estimation theory, substantial decision theory under uncertainty) and substantial methodological content about how to build substantial systems that work despite substantial uncertainty. The substantial contemporary work on this problem continues actively, with substantial recent advances in learning-based approaches that handle substantial uncertainty differently than traditional probabilistic approaches.

**The problem of substantial real-time computation.** Robots must operate in substantial real time — substantial decisions must be made on substantial timescales matching the substantial physical dynamics of the environment, with substantial implications throughout the systems engineering. The substantial difficulty arises because substantial perception, substantial planning, and substantial control all have substantial computational requirements, and substantial real-time constraints substantially shape what computations are feasible. The discipline includes substantial work on this problem: the substantial real-time systems engineering (substantially overlapping with Section 4.7), substantial work on substantial computational structures appropriate to robotics, substantial work on substantial hardware acceleration for robotics, substantial work on substantial trade-offs between substantial accuracy and substantial computational cost. The intellectual content includes substantial systems engineering content and substantial appreciation for what real-time constraints actually require.

**The problem of substantial integration across substantial subsystems.** Substantial robots have substantial subsystems — substantial sensors, substantial perception, substantial state estimation, substantial planning, substantial control, substantial actuation — and substantial integration across these subsystems is integral to substantial robot operation. The substantial difficulty arises because substantial subsystems have substantial dependencies on each other, substantial errors compound across substantial subsystems, and substantial integration problems can be substantially difficult to diagnose. The discipline includes substantial work on this problem: substantial work on substantial robot software architectures (ROS and the substantial various alternatives), substantial work on substantial system-level analysis and testing, substantial work on the substantial integration testing methodology that substantial robotics requires. The intellectual content includes substantial systems engineering and substantial methodological content.

**The problem of substantial physical interaction with substantial environments.** Robots interact physically with substantial environments — substantial environments push back, substantial contacts have substantial implications, substantial physical dynamics are substantial. The substantial difficulty arises because substantial physical interaction is substantially more complex than substantial pure computation, with substantial implications for what tasks robots can and cannot reliably perform. The discipline includes substantial work on this problem: substantial work on substantial contact-rich manipulation, substantial work on substantial compliant control, substantial work on substantial impedance control, substantial work on substantial physical interaction safety. The substantial contemporary work on substantial dexterous manipulation continues actively, with substantial recent advances through substantial learning-based approaches that handle substantial physical interaction differently than substantial traditional analytical approaches.

**The problem of substantial transfer from substantial simulation to substantial reality.** Substantial portions of substantial robotics development happen in substantial simulation — substantial training of substantial learned policies, substantial development of substantial planning algorithms, substantial testing of substantial system integration. But substantial deployment to substantial real robots routinely reveals substantial issues that substantial simulation did not capture: substantial sensor characteristics that substantial simulation modeled inadequately, substantial physical dynamics that substantial simulation approximated, substantial environmental variation that substantial simulation did not include. The substantial difficulty has substantial implications for substantial methodology. The discipline includes substantial work on this problem: substantial domain randomization (training in substantial varied simulated environments to produce policies robust to substantial reality), substantial system identification (calibrating substantial simulation to match substantial reality), substantial work on substantial sim-to-real transfer specifically, substantial work on substantial real-world testing methodology. The intellectual content includes substantial methodological content about how substantial simulation and substantial reality should be integrated in substantial development.

### Where it sits in the map

Robotics depends on substantial preparation across multiple regions of the map. The relationship to HCI (Section 6.4) is substantial through substantial human-robot interaction.

From outside computer science: the relationships are unusually substantial. Mechanical engineering provides substantial foundation for substantial robot mechanical design. Electrical engineering provides substantial foundation for substantial robot electrical systems. Control engineering provides substantial foundation for substantial control work — substantial portions of robotics control share substantial foundations with substantial broader control engineering. Mechatronics provides substantial integrative foundation. Mathematics broadly is substantial, with substantial robotics using substantial mathematics across substantial mathematical subdisciplines. Physics provides substantial foundation. Cognitive science and neuroscience are substantial through substantial work on substantial biologically-inspired robotics and substantial work on substantial cognitive architectures for robotics.

The position within the four axes of Chapter 1 is characteristic of application development: substantial engagement with the system axis (substantial embedded systems engineering), substantial engagement with the computation axis (substantial mathematical and algorithmic content), moderate engagement with the data axis (substantial sensor data engineering), and increasingly substantial engagement with the intelligence axis (substantial learning-based robotics, substantial foundation models for robotics). The discipline is substantially configurational and substantially integrative, with substantial integration across substantial CS axes and substantial integration with substantial non-CS engineering disciplines.

### What you become after studying it

A reader who has internalized robotics acquires several capacities that distinguish them from practitioners who treat robotics as collection of techniques.

The first is *substantial fluency with robotics mathematics*. The trained practitioner reasons fluently about substantial mathematics of robotics — substantial linear algebra in robotics contexts, substantial probability theory for substantial uncertainty, substantial control theory, substantial optimization. This mathematical fluency is foundational and broadly applicable, transferring across specific robotics contexts and remaining relevant as specific techniques evolve.

The second capacity is *substantial appreciation for substantial physical reality*. The trained practitioner takes substantial physical reality seriously as integral to robotics rather than as substantial abstract context. This appreciation reshapes how problems are approached: with substantial attention to substantial physical constraints, with substantial appreciation for what substantial physical interaction actually involves, with substantial humility about substantial gaps between substantial models and substantial reality. This appreciation is acquired through substantial practice with substantial physical robots and through substantial engagement with the substantial documented record of substantial sim-to-real failures.

The third capacity is *substantial integration of substantial perception, planning, and control*. The trained practitioner integrates substantial perception, substantial planning, and substantial control concerns rather than treating them as separable. This integrative capacity distinguishes substantial roboticists who can build substantial complete robotic systems from specialists in narrower subareas, and is acquired through substantial practice with substantial complete robot systems.

The fourth capacity is *substantial fluency with substantial probabilistic reasoning*. The trained practitioner has substantial fluency with substantial probabilistic reasoning under substantial uncertainty — substantial Bayesian estimation, substantial probabilistic planning, substantial appreciation for what substantial probability provides for substantial robotics. This probabilistic fluency is essential for substantial robotics work.

The fifth capacity is *substantial fluency with substantial real-time systems engineering*. The trained practitioner reasons naturally about substantial real-time constraints, can identify substantial bottlenecks in substantial robotic computational pipelines, can apply substantial various optimization techniques appropriate to substantial robotics contexts. This real-time fluency is essential for substantial robotic systems and is acquired through substantial practice.

The sixth capacity is *substantial appreciation for substantial diversity of substantial robotics application domains*. The trained practitioner recognizes that substantial industrial robotics, substantial autonomous vehicles, substantial manipulation, substantial mobile robotics, the substantial various other domains have substantial different requirements, and can adapt practice to specific application contexts.

The seventh capacity is *substantial fluency with substantial contemporary learning-based robotics*. The trained practitioner engages substantially with substantial recent developments in learning-based robotics, with substantial appreciation for what learning approaches do well and what they do poorly. This fluency is increasingly substantial as substantial portions of contemporary robotics involve substantial learning.

The eighth capacity is *substantial historical and conceptual perspective on substantial robotics*. The trained practitioner who has engaged with substantial robotics tradition has substantial perspective on substantial contemporary developments — understanding what approaches have been tried, what has succeeded, what has failed substantially repeatedly, what is genuinely new versus what is substantial recurring optimism. This perspective distinguishes substantial practitioners who engage with robotics as substantial intellectual discipline from substantial practitioners who engage with substantial contemporary techniques without substantial historical context.

The ninth capacity is *substantial appreciation for substantial broader implications*. The trained practitioner takes seriously substantial implications of robotic deployment beyond substantial technical performance — substantial implications for substantial workers and substantial labor, substantial safety implications for substantial people in substantial environments where robots operate, substantial broader societal implications. This appreciation distinguishes substantial roboticists who engage with substantial broader responsibilities from substantial practitioners who optimize narrowly for substantial immediate technical metrics.

### How the field sees the world

Robotics embeds a worldview that has emerged over substantial decades of substantial practice with substantial physical systems.

The first commitment is *to substantial physical reality as substantively important*. The robotics worldview holds that substantial engineering for substantial physical systems substantially differs from substantial engineering for substantial purely computational systems, with substantial physical reality producing substantial constraints that substantial pure computation does not face. This commitment runs against engineering dispositions that abstract over substantial physical concerns. The roboticist takes a different view: substantial physical concerns are integral, with substantial implications throughout substantial engineering.

The second commitment is *to substantial humility about substantial systems*. The robotics worldview takes seriously substantial documented record of substantial robotic systems failing in substantial unanticipated ways, of substantial promised capabilities not materializing, of substantial gap between substantial demonstration and substantial reliable operation. This commitment shapes practice: with substantial humility about substantial capability claims, with substantial respect for substantial difficulty of producing substantial reliable robotic systems, with substantial appreciation that substantial demonstration in substantial controlled conditions is substantially insufficient for substantial reliable deployment.

The third commitment is *to substantial integration as integral engineering*. The robotics worldview holds that substantial integration across substantial subsystems is integral engineering work rather than auxiliary. This commitment shapes how problems are approached: with substantial attention to substantial subsystem interactions, with substantial appreciation that substantial errors compound across substantial subsystems, with substantial recognition that substantial integration testing is essential.

The fourth commitment is *to substantial probabilistic thinking under substantial uncertainty*. The robotics worldview takes seriously substantial uncertainty about substantial sensor measurements, substantial environment models, substantial action outcomes, with substantial implications for how engineering must proceed. This commitment shapes practice: with substantial probabilistic reasoning throughout, with substantial appreciation for what substantial probability provides for substantial robotics, with substantial humility about substantial deterministic models.

The fifth commitment is *to substantial empirical engineering*. The robotics worldview takes seriously that substantial portions of substantial effective practice depend on substantial empirical observations about what substantial works in substantial reality, that substantial theoretical reasoning often substantially underdetermines what substantial deployment requires, and that substantial methodology depends on substantial empirical observation including substantial real-world testing. This commitment runs against engineering dispositions that prefer pure simulation. Practitioners who have absorbed it treat substantial real-world testing as integral.

The sixth commitment is *to substantial real-time engineering*. The robotics worldview takes seriously substantial real-time constraints as substantively important rather than as substantial implementation detail. This commitment shapes how problems are approached: with substantial attention to substantial computational requirements, with substantial appreciation for what real-time constraints actually require, with substantial methodology for measuring and addressing real-time concerns.

The seventh commitment is *to substantial cross-disciplinary engagement*. The robotics worldview takes substantial cross-disciplinary engagement seriously — with substantial mechanical engineering, substantial electrical engineering, substantial control engineering, substantial mathematics, substantial physics. This commitment runs against engineering dispositions that prefer to remain within computer science narrowly understood. Practitioners who have absorbed this commitment engage substantially with adjacent engineering disciplines as integral to substantial robotics practice.

The eighth commitment is *to substantial appreciation for substantial cycles in robotics history*. The robotics worldview takes seriously substantial cycles of substantial enthusiasm and substantial disappointment that have characterized substantial robotics development, with substantial implications for how substantial contemporary claims should be evaluated. Practitioners who have absorbed this commitment engage with substantial contemporary developments substantively rather than reactively, recognizing that substantial cycles are likely to continue.

The ninth commitment is *to substantial responsibility for substantial deployment*. The robotics worldview takes seriously substantial implications of substantial robotic deployment for substantial people who interact with substantial robots — substantial workers in substantial industrial contexts, substantial people in substantial spaces where substantial autonomous vehicles operate, the substantial various other people affected by substantial deployment. This commitment shapes how engineering proceeds, with substantial attention to substantial safety concerns, substantial ergonomic concerns, the substantial various other concerns that substantial deployment introduces.

These nine commitments — to physical reality, to humility about systems, to integration as integral engineering, to probabilistic thinking, to empirical engineering, to real-time engineering, to cross-disciplinary engagement, to appreciation for cycles in robotics history, and to responsibility for deployment — distinguish the robotics worldview from adjacent dispositions. Practitioners who have absorbed them work in robotics as substantial professional engineering practice rather than as substantial application of demos.

### Common pitfalls in learning it

The pitfalls of learning robotics are characteristic and substantially distinctive — the substantial integration character, the substantial mathematical content, and the substantial gap between substantial simulation and substantial reality produce many opportunities for misdirected learning.

The most common pitfall is *the simulation-only practice*. Substantial robotics development happens in simulation, and learners can come to develop substantial simulation competence while remaining substantially unprepared for substantial real-world deployment. The result is substantial work that performs well in simulation but fails substantially in deployment. The countermeasure is substantial engagement with real robots: substantial work with real hardware substantially supplementing substantial simulation work, substantial appreciation for substantial sim-to-real gaps, substantial methodology that integrates substantial simulation and substantial real-world testing.

A second pitfall is *the math-avoidance trap*. Robotics has substantial mathematical content — substantial linear algebra, substantial probability theory, substantial control theory, substantial optimization — and learners can come to avoid the mathematics, relying on libraries to handle the mathematical content. The result is practitioners who can use robotic libraries for standard tasks but cannot reason about what they are doing, cannot adapt techniques to non-standard problems, cannot diagnose issues whose cause is mathematical. The countermeasure is to engage with the mathematics seriously: to study the substantial probabilistic robotics, the substantial control theory, the substantial linear algebra in robotics contexts, recognizing that substantial robotics competence substantially requires substantial mathematical preparation.

A third pitfall is *the perception-only or planning-only or control-only specialization without integration*. Substantial robotics requires substantial integration across substantial subsystems, and learners can come to specialize narrowly in one subsystem without substantial appreciation for how it fits into substantial complete systems. The result is substantial work that performs well in isolation but fails to integrate. The countermeasure is substantial work on substantial complete robotic systems: substantial integration of substantial perception, planning, and control even when substantial primary specialization is narrow, substantial appreciation for substantial subsystem interactions.

A fourth pitfall is *the demo-driven understanding without engineering depth*. Robotics has substantial visible demonstrations — substantial videos of substantial impressive demonstrations widely circulate — and learners can come to overestimate substantial robotic capabilities based on demonstrations without substantial engineering context. The substantial gap between substantial cherry-picked demonstrations and substantial reliable deployment is substantial. The countermeasure is substantial engineering engagement: substantial appreciation for what reliable deployment actually requires, substantial humility about what demonstrations show, substantial engagement with substantial documented record of substantial systems that demonstrated well but deployed poorly.

A fifth pitfall is *the framework-as-discipline misconception applied to ROS*. Robot Operating System (ROS) is substantial software framework for robotics, and learners can come to treat learning ROS as the substance of learning robotics. The result is practitioners who can use ROS for standard tasks but cannot reason about substantial underlying concepts. The countermeasure is to engage with substantial concepts that ROS implements: to study substantial messaging and substantial computation graph concepts, to engage with substantial alternative architectures, to recognize that ROS knowledge is substantially valuable but substantially insufficient as foundation.

A sixth pitfall is *the under-appreciation of substantial physical intuition*. Substantial robotics requires substantial physical intuition — substantial appreciation for substantial mechanical behavior, substantial appreciation for substantial sensor characteristics, substantial appreciation for substantial real-world dynamics. Learners can come to develop substantial computational competence without substantial physical intuition. The countermeasure is substantial hands-on work with substantial physical systems: substantial building of robots, substantial debugging of real hardware, substantial development of substantial physical intuition through substantial direct engagement.

A seventh pitfall is *the safety-as-afterthought disposition*. Substantial robots can substantially harm substantial people — substantial industrial robots have substantial track record of substantial industrial accidents, substantial autonomous vehicles have substantial documented incidents, substantial collaborative robots have substantial safety concerns. Learners can come to treat safety as concern to be addressed after primary engineering, with substantial result that substantial safety has substantial gaps. The countermeasure is to integrate safety from substantial start of substantial robotics work: substantial study of substantial robotic safety standards, substantial appreciation for substantial safety implications of substantial robot deployment, substantial methodology that treats safety as integral.

An eighth pitfall is *the optimism-driven practice*. Robotics has substantial history of substantial optimistic claims that substantially fail to materialize, and learners can come to absorb substantial optimism without substantial historical context. The result is substantial work that overpromises and substantially underdelivers. The countermeasure is substantial historical engagement: substantial study of substantial robotics history including substantial documented failures, substantial appreciation for substantial cycles of enthusiasm and disappointment, substantial humility about substantial contemporary capability claims.

A ninth pitfall is *the LLM-shortcut problem* as applied to robotics. Modern AI tools can produce substantial robotics code, can answer substantial robotics questions. The cost is substantial in robotics because substantial competent practice depends on substantial physical intuition that develops through hands-on engagement, on substantial integration competence that develops through substantial complete-system work, on substantial appreciation for substantial reality versus simulation that develops through substantial real-world testing. The countermeasure is to use AI tools as supplements to substantial direct engagement rather than as replacements.

A tenth pitfall is *the substantial AI-everything trap*. Substantial recent enthusiasm for substantial learning-based robotics can produce substantial expectation that substantial machine learning will solve substantially all robotics problems, with substantial result that substantial classical approaches are substantially neglected when they would substantially better serve specific applications. The substantial classical robotics approaches (substantial planning algorithms, substantial classical control, substantial probabilistic estimation, the substantial various others) substantially work well for substantial application categories. The countermeasure is substantial appreciation for substantial appropriate use of substantial different approaches: substantial classical methods for substantial problems where they work well, substantial learning methods where they substantially provide unique value, substantial integration of approaches when integration substantially benefits.

An eleventh pitfall is *the deployment-naive practice*. Substantial robotics research can proceed without substantial engagement with substantial deployment realities — substantial cost concerns, substantial maintenance concerns, substantial regulatory concerns, the substantial various deployment realities. Learners can come to develop substantial research competence without substantial deployment understanding. The countermeasure is substantial engagement with substantial deployment: substantial attention to substantial deployment concerns from substantial early in learning, substantial appreciation for what substantial deployment actually requires beyond substantial technical performance.

### The literature of the subject

The literature on robotics is substantial and has substantial diverse character. The discipline has substantial canonical texts that remain substantively valuable, substantial contemporary literature addressing substantial recent developments particularly in learning-based robotics, and substantial diversity across substantial robotics subspecialties. Engagement requires substantial fluency with this diverse literature.

#### Canonical entry points

The substantial canonical introduction to robotics as integrated discipline is Siciliano, Sciavicco, Villani, and Oriolo's *Robotics: Modelling, Planning and Control* (2009). The book covers substantial breadth of foundational robotics with substantial mathematical depth, providing substantial preparation for serious robotics study. The book has substantial canonical status across substantial robotics programs.

For probabilistic robotics specifically, Thrun, Burgard, and Fox's *Probabilistic Robotics* (2005) is the standard text. The book substantially covers substantial probabilistic foundations of robotics with substantial depth, particularly substantial work on substantial state estimation and substantial localization. The book remains substantively foundational despite age, with substantial subsequent literature building on its foundations.

For control specifically, Spong, Hutchinson, and Vidyasagar's *Robot Modeling and Control* (second edition, 2020) covers substantial robot control with substantial depth. Slotine and Li's *Applied Nonlinear Control* covers broader nonlinear control that substantial robotics depends on substantially.

For modern robotics with contemporary perspective, Lynch and Park's *Modern Robotics: Mechanics, Planning, and Control* (2017, freely available online) provides substantial accessible introduction with substantial mathematical care. The book is unusually well-pedagogically structured and is freely available with substantial accompanying software, making it substantial appropriate primary text for substantial contemporary robotics study.

For motion planning specifically, LaValle's *Planning Algorithms* (2006, freely available) is substantial. The book substantially covers substantial planning at substantial depth with substantial mathematical care.

For computer vision in robotics specifically, Hartley and Zisserman's *Multiple View Geometry in Computer Vision* (second edition, 2003) covers substantial geometric computer vision foundational for substantial visual perception in robotics. Szeliski's *Computer Vision: Algorithms and Applications* (mentioned in Section 5.2) provides substantial broader computer vision coverage.

For learners interested in robotic manipulation specifically, Mason's *Mechanics of Robotic Manipulation* covers substantial manipulation foundations. Murray, Li, and Sastry's *A Mathematical Introduction to Robotic Manipulation* (1994, freely available) covers substantial mathematical foundations of manipulation.

The reading-order recommendation depends substantially on focus area. For comprehensive foundation: Lynch and Park as foundational text given accessibility and freely available status, supplemented by Siciliano et al. and Thrun et al. for depth in specific areas. For learners pursuing specific subareas: substantial specialty-specific resources alongside foundational material.

The various robotics courses at major universities (MIT 6.832, Stanford CS223A, CMU 16-XXX, Berkeley CS287, the substantial various others) provide substantial pedagogical material with substantial freely available content. The substantial Coursera and edX robotics specializations provide substantial structured curriculum.

#### The progression

After the canonical introduction, the progression in robotics branches according to specialization.

*Kinematics and dynamics at depth.* For learners pursuing kinematics and dynamics seriously, Featherstone's *Rigid Body Dynamics Algorithms* covers substantial efficient algorithms. The substantial papers on Lie group formulations of robot kinematics extend substantial mathematical depth.

*Control at depth.* Beyond Spong et al. and Slotine-Li, the substantial broader control literature is relevant. Khalil's *Nonlinear Systems* covers substantial nonlinear control. The various optimal control texts cover substantial optimal control. The various MPC (Model Predictive Control) literature covers substantial MPC for robotics. The substantial papers on contemporary substantial reinforcement-learning-based control extend the picture.

*Probabilistic robotics at depth.* Beyond Thrun et al., the substantial subsequent literature on Bayesian filtering, on substantial particle filters, on substantial graph-based SLAM (Grisetti et al. and the substantial various others), on substantial visual-inertial estimation extends the picture. Stachniss's online courses provide substantial educational complement.

*SLAM at depth.* Cadena et al.'s substantial "Past, Present, and Future of SLAM" survey covers substantial SLAM evolution. The substantial papers on substantial graph-based SLAM (g2o, GTSAM, the substantial various others), on substantial visual SLAM (ORB-SLAM and the substantial subsequent work), on substantial LiDAR SLAM constitute substantial deeper material.

*Motion planning at depth.* Beyond LaValle, the substantial papers on substantial RRT and substantial PRM variants, on substantial trajectory optimization (CHOMP, TrajOpt, the substantial various others), on substantial planning under uncertainty constitute substantial deeper material.

*Manipulation at depth.* Beyond Mason and Murray-Li-Sastry, the substantial recent literature on substantial dexterous manipulation, on substantial learning-based manipulation, on substantial bimanual manipulation, on the substantial various manipulation challenges constitutes substantial active research.

The substantial papers on substantial wheeled robots, on substantial legged robots (the substantial Boston Dynamics work, the substantial ANYmal work, the substantial subsequent work), on substantial flying robots (drones, the substantial various others) extend the picture.

*Autonomous vehicles at depth.* The substantial autonomous vehicles literature is substantial. Substantial work on substantial perception for autonomous vehicles, on substantial behavior prediction, on substantial planning for autonomous driving extends the picture.

*Robot learning at depth.* The substantial recent literature on robot learning is rapidly developing. *Foundation models for robotics at depth.* The substantial recent literature on substantial foundation models for robotics is rapidly developing. The substantial RT-1, RT-2 papers from Google DeepMind, the substantial PaLM-E paper, the substantial various contemporary papers on substantial vision-language-action models, the substantial various papers on substantial code-generating models for robotics constitute the substantial recent literature.

*Human-robot interaction at depth.* The substantial HRI literature has substantial breadth. Goodrich and Schultz's "Human-Robot Interaction: A Survey" provides substantial introduction. The substantial papers on substantial collaborative robots (cobots), on substantial assistive robotics, on substantial social robotics extend the picture.

The substantial intersection with multi-agent systems and with multi-agent reinforcement learning is substantial.

*Robot software architectures at depth.* Beyond the substantial ROS documentation, substantial papers on substantial robot software architectures, on substantial real-time robotics, on substantial systems engineering for robotics constitute substantial literature. ROS 2 documentation extends ROS substantially.

*Specific application domains at depth.* Each substantial application domain has substantial sub-literature. Industrial robotics has substantial literature on substantial industrial robot programming, substantial collaborative robotics, substantial flexible automation. Surgical robotics has substantial literature on substantial surgical robot design and substantial clinical applications. Agricultural robotics, construction robotics, the substantial various other domains each have their own sub-literatures.
