#### Heterodox correctives

Several positions are heterodox correctives within robotics and worth engagement for the perspective they provide.

*The skeptical perspective on autonomous vehicle timelines.* Substantial work questions whether substantial autonomous vehicle deployment will materialize on substantial timelines that have been substantially repeatedly promised. The substantial various papers and writings on substantial autonomous driving difficulties, on substantial documented failures, on substantial gap between substantial demonstration and substantial reliable deployment constitute substantial heterodox literature.

*The substantial classical robotics perspective on substantial learning-based enthusiasm.* Substantial practitioners argue that substantial recent enthusiasm for learning-based robotics has substantially overshadowed substantial classical robotics methods that substantially work better for substantial application categories. The substantial argument has substantial proponents and substantial intellectual content. Engagement with this perspective gives substantial perspective on what substantial appropriate use of substantial different approaches looks like.

*The embodiment-centric perspective.* Substantial work in robotics emphasizes that substantial intelligence is fundamentally embodied — that substantial cognitive capabilities arise substantially from substantial physical interaction with substantial environments rather than from substantial disembodied computation. Brooks's "Intelligence Without Reason" (mentioned in Section 5.5) substantially articulates this perspective, and substantial subsequent work has developed it. Engagement gives substantial perspective on substantial relationship between substantial embodiment and substantial intelligence.

*The "robotics has been overpromised for substantial decades" perspective.* The substantial documented record of substantial robotic capability claims that substantially failed to materialize has produced substantial skeptical literature. The substantial historical reviews and substantial critical writings constitute substantial heterodox literature. The substantial implications for substantial how substantial contemporary claims should be evaluated are substantial.

*The labor and political economy perspective.* Substantial work questions substantial implications of robotic deployment for substantial workers and substantial broader political economy. The substantial papers on substantial labor displacement, on substantial concentration of substantial robotic ownership, on substantial broader political implications constitute substantial heterodox literature with substantial intellectual content.

*The "sim-to-real has fundamental limitations" perspective.* Various practitioners argue that substantial sim-to-real transfer has substantial fundamental limitations that substantial methodology cannot fully address. The substantial argument has substantial implications for what substantial development methodology should look like.

*The disability studies perspective on assistive robotics.* Substantial work questions substantial assumptions of substantial assistive robotics — questioning whether substantial robotic solutions substantially serve substantial disabled people or substantially serve substantial others' assumptions about what disabled people need. Engagement with substantial disability studies literature gives substantial perspective on substantial design assumptions.

#### What to skip and why

Books on robotics published before approximately 2010 should be approached as historical references rather than as guides to contemporary practice, with substantial exceptions of foundational books (Murray-Li-Sastry, Mason, the substantial various others that retain substantial value).

Marketing-driven books on substantial future robotics that conflate substantial speculation with substantial engineering should be approached with substantial caution. Substantial documented record of substantial unfulfilled robotic promises makes substantial speculative writing about substantial robotics future substantially unreliable.

Books on specific old robotic platforms or substantial old robotic frameworks are substantially obsolete for substantial contemporary practice.

Books on robotics that abstract over substantial mathematics should be approached with substantial caution. Substantial robotics has substantial mathematical content that substantial serious practice substantially requires.

Most online robotics tutorials are uneven in quality. The exceptions — the substantial well-maintained Lynch-Park course materials, the substantial substantial ROS tutorials, the substantial talks from substantial robotics conferences — are valuable.

Books that present substantial autonomous vehicles as substantially solved problem should be approached with substantial caution given substantial documented difficulties.

Books on AI/robotics that anthropomorphize substantial robots without substantial grounding should be approached with substantial caution.

Pre-foundation-model books on robotic learning are substantially dated for substantial contemporary practice.

### A note on contemporary relevance

Robotics is among the more contemporarily active subjects on the application axis, with substantial developments substantially reshaping the discipline.

The most consequential contemporary development is the substantial integration of substantial foundation models with substantial robotics. The substantial work on substantial vision-language-action models (RT-2, PaLM-E, the substantial various subsequent work), on substantial code-generating models for robotics, on substantial general-purpose robot policies represents substantial recent advances. The trajectory suggests continued substantial foundation-model-robotics integration.

The second major development is the substantial maturation of substantial humanoid robotics. Substantial recent advances by substantial Boston Dynamics, substantial Tesla Optimus, substantial Figure, substantial various Chinese humanoid robot developers have substantially advanced substantial humanoid capabilities. The substantial commercial trajectory remains genuinely uncertain.

The third development is the substantial reckoning with substantial autonomous vehicle timelines. Substantial autonomous vehicles have substantially deployed in substantial limited contexts (substantial Waymo robotaxis in substantial limited cities, substantial Tesla Autopilot in supervised contexts) but substantial broader deployment has substantially repeatedly slipped. The substantial trajectory remains genuinely contested.

The fourth development is the substantial growth of substantial industrial collaborative robotics. Substantial cobots have substantially expanded substantial industrial robotic deployment beyond substantial traditional caged industrial robots. The trajectory suggests continued substantial cobot growth.

The fifth development is the substantial maturation of substantial drone technology and substantial commercial drone applications. Substantial drone deployment in substantial agriculture, substantial inspection, substantial delivery (with substantial caveats), the substantial various other applications has substantially advanced.

The sixth development is the substantial growth of substantial concerns about substantial autonomous weapons. Substantial weaponization of substantial robotic systems, substantial development of substantial autonomous weapon systems, substantial broader concerns about substantial AI in warfare have substantially developed. The substantial implications remain genuinely contested.

The seventh development is the substantial maturation of substantial simulation environments for robotics. Substantial Isaac Sim, substantial various other simulation environments, substantial advances in substantial sim-to-real transfer have substantially advanced what substantial simulation can do for robotics development.

The eighth development is the substantial growing engagement with substantial labor implications of robotic deployment. Substantial concerns about substantial automation displacing substantial workers, substantial broader political economy concerns, substantial movements toward substantial robotic deployment serving substantial workers rather than displacing them constitute substantial substantial active discussion.

Despite these developments, the foundational character of robotics is substantially robust. The core concerns — operating under uncertainty, real-time computation, integration across subsystems, physical interaction, simulation-to-reality transfer — remain as articulated decades ago. The contemporary developments add to this foundation rather than replacing it.

A final observation specific to robotics: among the application chapters, this is the one with the most substantial cycles of substantial enthusiasm and substantial disappointment. Substantial robotic capabilities have been substantially repeatedly promised and substantially repeatedly underdelivered, with substantial implications for how substantial contemporary claims should be evaluated. Practitioners pursuing substantial competence in this area should engage with substantial historical perspective substantively, recognizing that substantial cycles likely continue and that substantial contemporary claims warrant substantial engineering scrutiny rather than reactive belief or reactive dismissal.

A second observation specific to this subject: robotics has substantial unique character among engineering disciplines through substantial integration of substantial computer science with substantial mechanical, electrical, and control engineering. The substantial integration produces substantial intellectual richness but also substantial difficulty — substantial competent practice substantially requires substantial fluency across substantial different engineering disciplines, and substantial portions of substantial robotics organizations struggle with substantial integration. Practitioners pursuing substantial robotics competence should engage substantially with this integration as substantial central concern.

A third observation specific to this subject: substantial robotics is substantial discipline where substantial gap between substantial demonstration and substantial deployment remains substantial. Substantial videos of substantial impressive robotic demonstrations widely circulate, but substantial reliable deployment in substantial real-world contexts remains substantially difficult. Practitioners pursuing substantial robotics competence should engage substantially with this gap, developing substantial appreciation for what substantial reliable deployment requires beyond substantial demonstration capabilities.
## 6.6 — Scientific Computing

### What it is, properly

Scientific computing is the discipline concerned with computational methods for substantial scientific and engineering problems — substantial numerical methods for substantial mathematical problems that arise in substantial scientific work, substantial simulation of substantial physical systems, substantial computational analysis of substantial scientific data, and substantial integration of computation with substantial scientific research practice. The subject treats computation as substantial intellectual partner to substantial scientific inquiry, with characteristic mathematical content (substantial numerical analysis, substantial linear algebra, substantial differential equations, substantial probability and statistics), characteristic engineering content (substantial high-performance computing, substantial parallel and distributed computing, substantial numerical software engineering), characteristic application domains (computational physics, computational chemistry, computational biology, climate modeling, the substantial various scientific domains), and substantial methodological concerns specific to substantial scientific work (substantial reproducibility, substantial verification and validation, substantial uncertainty quantification). The discipline has substantial historical depth, with substantial development from the 1940s onward through substantial early scientific computing on substantial early computers, substantial subsequent development through substantial supercomputing era and substantial maturation of substantial numerical libraries, and substantial contemporary development driven substantially by substantial machine learning integration with substantial scientific computing and by substantial growth of substantial data-intensive science.

The conventional account treats scientific computing as essentially the practice of "writing programs that scientists use" — substantial computational implementations of substantial scientific procedures, substantial data analysis scripts, substantial simulation code. This account captures the surface activity but understates substantially what scientific computing has become. The conceptual core is the recognition that *substantial scientific computing involves substantial mathematical content beyond surface mechanics*, that *substantial numerical methods have substantial subtleties that substantial naive implementation routinely violates*, that *substantial computational science requires substantial methodological discipline that substantial software development alone does not provide*, that *substantial integration of substantial computation with substantial scientific work involves substantial considerations specific to substantial scientific contexts*, and that *substantial contemporary scientific computing increasingly substantially intersects with substantial machine learning in substantial ways that substantially transform substantial portions of practice*. These recognitions distinguish substantial scientific computing as serious discipline from substantial casual scripting that some practitioners may mistake for it.

A more honest account distinguishes several distinct concerns within the broader subject. *Numerical analysis* is the substantial subdiscipline concerned with substantial mathematical analysis of substantial numerical methods — substantial work on substantial accuracy and substantial stability of substantial algorithms, substantial work on substantial conditioning of substantial problems, substantial work on substantial floating-point arithmetic and substantial implications throughout numerical computation. *Numerical linear algebra* covers the substantial mathematical and computational work on substantial linear algebra problems — substantial work on substantial direct methods (substantial LU, substantial QR, substantial SVD, the substantial various others), substantial work on substantial iterative methods (substantial Krylov subspace methods, substantial various others), substantial work on substantial sparse linear algebra, substantial work on substantial parallel linear algebra. *Numerical methods for differential equations* covers substantial computational solution of substantial differential equations — substantial ODE methods (substantial explicit and substantial implicit methods, substantial various time integration schemes), substantial PDE methods (substantial finite difference, substantial finite element, substantial finite volume, substantial spectral methods), substantial work on substantial stiff problems and substantial various challenging cases. *Numerical optimization* covers substantial computational solution of substantial optimization problems — substantial unconstrained and constrained optimization, substantial linear and nonlinear programming, substantial work on substantial various algorithmic approaches. *Statistical and probabilistic computation* covers substantial computational methods for substantial statistical and probabilistic problems — substantial Monte Carlo methods, substantial Markov chain Monte Carlo, substantial various sampling methods, substantial work on substantial Bayesian computation. *High-performance scientific computing* covers substantial systems engineering of substantial scientific computation at substantial scale — substantially overlapping with the HPC material in Section 4.8 but with substantial scientific-computing-specific concerns. *Numerical software engineering* covers substantial engineering of substantial numerical software — substantial scientific software libraries (substantial LAPACK, substantial PETSc, substantial Trilinos, substantial various others), substantial work on substantial software architecture for substantial scientific computing, substantial work on substantial verification and validation of substantial scientific software. *Computational science domains* — computational physics, computational chemistry, computational biology, computational fluid dynamics, climate modeling, the substantial various others — each have substantial domain-specific concerns alongside substantial general principles. *Scientific machine learning* is the increasingly substantial subdiscipline concerned with substantial integration of substantial machine learning with substantial scientific computing — substantial physics-informed neural networks, substantial neural surrogate models, substantial work on substantial machine learning for substantial scientific discovery. *Data-intensive science* covers substantial computational concerns of substantial science with substantial data — substantial data management at substantial scale, substantial data analysis pipelines, substantial reproducibility concerns, substantial computational concerns specific to substantial data-intensive scientific work. *Reproducibility and computational provenance* covers the substantial methodological concerns of substantial computational science specifically — substantial work on substantial reproducible computation, substantial workflow management, substantial computational provenance tracking. *Visualization for science* substantially overlaps with the visualization material in Section 6.2 but with substantial scientific-computing-specific concerns.

The intellectual content of scientific computing is distinctive in several ways. The mathematical foundation is substantial — substantial portions of scientific computing depend on substantial mathematics including substantial numerical analysis, substantial linear algebra, substantial calculus, substantial differential equations, substantial probability theory, with substantial preparation required for substantial competent practice. The substantial connection to substantial established scientific disciplines is substantial — substantial scientific computing serves substantial scientific work and substantial competent practice substantially requires engagement with substantial scientific domains. The substantial methodological concerns are substantial — substantial reproducibility, substantial verification and validation, substantial uncertainty quantification, the substantial various methodological concerns are integral rather than auxiliary. The substantial software engineering character is substantial — substantial scientific computing involves substantial software development with substantial engineering concerns, but with substantial concerns specific to substantial scientific contexts that substantial general software engineering does not directly address. The substantial integration with substantial machine learning is substantial and rapidly developing — substantial portions of substantial contemporary scientific computing substantially involve substantial machine learning, with substantial implications for what substantial practice now requires.

The reason this account matters is that scientific computing has become substantially central to substantial portions of contemporary science. Substantial scientific work depends substantially on substantial computational methods; substantial scientific discovery increasingly depends on substantial computational capabilities; substantial scientific software constitutes substantial scientific infrastructure that substantial science depends on. The gap between informed scientific computing practice and uninformed practice is substantial, and the substantial cost of the gap falls substantially on substantial scientific work. The discipline of scientific computing as serious technical practice differs substantially from substantial casual scientific scripting, and competent work requires substantial preparation that substantial scientific training alone often does not provide.

### The deep problems the field tries to answer

Five problems organize scientific computing as a discipline.

**The problem of substantial numerical accuracy and stability.** Substantial computational methods compute approximations rather than exact answers, and substantial accuracy of substantial approximations depends substantially on substantial properties of substantial methods and substantial properties of substantial problems. The substantial difficulty arises because naive implementation routinely produces substantial inaccuracy that substantial users do not notice — substantial floating-point arithmetic introduces substantial errors that substantially compound, substantial ill-conditioned problems substantially amplify substantial errors, substantial unstable algorithms substantially produce substantial unreliable results. The discipline includes substantial work on this problem: substantial numerical analysis tradition (Higham's substantial *Accuracy and Stability of Numerical Algorithms*, the substantial various canonical numerical analysis texts), substantial work on substantial conditioning analysis, substantial work on substantial backward and forward error analysis, substantial work on substantial stable algorithms and substantial substitutes for substantial unstable ones. The intellectual content includes substantial mathematical content about substantial errors and substantial methodological content about substantial how to produce substantial reliable computational results.

**The problem of substantial computation at substantial scale for substantial science.** Substantial scientific computation can substantially require substantial computational resources — substantial climate models run on substantial supercomputers, substantial molecular dynamics simulations require substantial parallel computation, substantial particle physics analyses process substantial data at substantial scale. The substantial difficulty arises because substantial scaling computation introduces substantial concerns: substantial parallel programming, substantial memory hierarchy concerns, substantial communication concerns in substantial distributed computation, substantial various performance optimization concerns. The discipline includes substantial work on this problem (substantially overlapping with HPC material in Section 4.8): substantial parallel scientific computing libraries (substantial PETSc, substantial Trilinos, substantial others), substantial work on substantial parallel algorithms specific to scientific computing, substantial work on substantial GPU computing for science, substantial work on substantial heterogeneous computing for substantial science. The intellectual content includes substantial systems engineering and substantial algorithmic content specific to scientific computing at scale.

**The problem of substantial reproducibility and verification of substantial computational science.** Substantial scientific work substantially depends on substantial reproducibility — substantial results should be verifiable by substantial others, substantial computational procedures should be substantially reproducible. The substantial difficulty arises because substantial computational science has substantial documented reproducibility problems — substantial computational results can depend on substantial software versions, on substantial hardware specifics, on substantial numerical details that substantial papers do not adequately document, on substantial random seeds, on the substantial various other factors. The discipline includes substantial work on this problem: substantial reproducible computing tools (substantial containerization, substantial workflow management systems, substantial various reproducibility infrastructure), substantial methodological work on substantial reproducible computational science practice, substantial work on substantial software preservation for substantial scientific software. The intellectual content includes substantial methodological content and substantial appreciation for substantial what reliable computational science actually requires.

**The problem of substantial uncertainty quantification.** Substantial scientific computation produces substantial results with substantial uncertainty — substantial uncertainty in substantial input data, substantial uncertainty in substantial models, substantial uncertainty in substantial numerical methods. The substantial difficulty arises in substantially quantifying substantial uncertainty in substantial computational results, with substantial implications for what substantial computational results actually establish. The discipline includes substantial work on this problem: substantial work on substantial probabilistic uncertainty quantification, substantial work on substantial sensitivity analysis, substantial work on substantial Monte Carlo methods for uncertainty quantification, substantial work on substantial Bayesian computation for substantial scientific inference. The intellectual content is substantial and increasingly substantial as substantial scientific computing matures.

**The problem of substantial integration of substantial computation with substantial scientific practice.** Substantial scientific computing serves substantial scientific work, and substantial integration with substantial scientific practice involves substantial considerations beyond substantial purely technical concerns. The substantial difficulty arises because substantial scientific work has substantial methodological standards, substantial publication conventions, substantial substantial review practices that substantial computational components must integrate with. The discipline includes substantial work on this problem: substantial work on substantial scientific software publication and substantial software citation, substantial work on substantial computational scientific workflows, substantial work on substantial collaboration between substantial computational scientists and substantial domain scientists, substantial work on substantial training of substantial scientists in substantial computational methods. The intellectual content is substantial and substantially overlaps with substantial broader concerns about substantial scientific practice in substantial computational age.

### Where it sits in the map

Scientific computing depends on substantial preparation across multiple regions of the map. From the foundational trunk: algorithms and data structures (Section 2.7) provides foundation, particularly substantial work on substantial graph algorithms and substantial data structures used substantially in substantial scientific applications. From the mathematical substrate (Chapter 7): substantial mathematical preparation is essential and substantially extensive. Calculus, particularly multivariable calculus, is foundational. Differential equations are foundational for substantial portions of scientific computing. Probability theory is foundational for substantial statistical and uncertainty quantification work. Optimization theory is foundational. From the systems axis: the engagement is substantial. High-performance computing (Section 4.8) is substantially relevant — substantial scientific computing is substantially HPC work. Modern compilers and language runtimes (Section 4.6) are substantially relevant through substantial scientific computing's substantial use of substantial compiler infrastructure and substantial various language runtimes. Modern databases (Section 4.2) are substantially relevant through substantial data-intensive scientific work.

From the intelligence axis: the engagement has grown substantially with substantial recent scientific machine learning developments. Machine learning foundations (Section 5.1), deep learning (Section 5.2), and increasingly foundation models (Section 5.3) are substantially relevant for substantial contemporary scientific computing.

From the data axis: the engagement is substantial through substantial scientific data engineering and substantial data-intensive scientific computation.

From other application domains: the relationship to graphics (Section 6.2) is substantial through substantial scientific visualization. The relationship to security engineering (Section 6.3) is moderately substantial through substantial concerns about substantial scientific software and substantial computational integrity.

From outside computer science: the relationships are unusually substantial. Mathematics broadly is foundational — substantial scientific computing uses substantial mathematics across substantial mathematical subdisciplines. The substantial scientific disciplines that scientific computing serves are substantively integral — substantial competent practice in scientific computing for physics requires substantial physics, for chemistry requires substantial chemistry, for biology requires substantial biology, the substantial various others. Engineering disciplines that use substantial scientific computing (substantial mechanical engineering, substantial chemical engineering, substantial civil engineering, the substantial various others) have substantial connections. Applied mathematics has unusually substantial connection — substantial portions of scientific computing substantially overlap with substantial applied mathematics.

The position within the four axes of Chapter 1 is characteristic of application development with substantial difference: substantial engagement with the system axis (substantial HPC engineering), substantial engagement with the computation axis (substantial mathematical and algorithmic content), substantial engagement with the data axis (substantial scientific data work), and increasingly substantial engagement with the intelligence axis (substantial scientific machine learning). The discipline is substantially configurational and substantially integrative, with substantial integration across substantial CS axes and substantial integration with substantial scientific disciplines that serve as substantial substantive content.

### What you become after studying it

A reader who has internalized scientific computing acquires several capacities that distinguish them from practitioners who treat scientific computing as casual scripting.

The first is *substantial fluency with substantial numerical methods*. The trained practitioner is fluent with substantial standard numerical methods: substantial linear algebra algorithms, substantial methods for differential equations, substantial optimization methods, substantial Monte Carlo methods, the substantial various others. The fluency includes understanding what each method does, what assumptions it makes, what its accuracy and stability properties are, what alternatives exist. This fluency is essential for substantial scientific computing work and is acquired through substantial study and substantial practice.

The second capacity is *substantial appreciation for substantial numerical accuracy and stability*. ### How the field sees the world

Scientific computing embeds a worldview that has emerged over substantial decades of substantial integration of substantial computation with substantial scientific work.

The first commitment is *to substantial numerical care as substantively important*. The scientific computing worldview holds that substantial numerical computation requires substantial care — substantial appreciation for substantial floating-point arithmetic, substantial appreciation for substantial conditioning and stability, substantial methodological discipline about substantial accuracy. This commitment runs against engineering dispositions that treat numerical concerns as substantial implementation detail. The scientific computing practitioner takes a different view: substantial numerical concerns are integral, with substantial implications for whether substantial computational results are substantively reliable.

The second commitment is *to substantial mathematical foundations as integral*. The scientific computing worldview takes seriously that substantial scientific computing requires substantial mathematics, and that substantial mathematical fluency is integral to substantial competent practice. This commitment runs against engineering dispositions that abstract over substantial mathematical content. Practitioners who have absorbed this commitment treat mathematical engagement as substantively required rather than as nice-to-have.

The third commitment is *to substantial reproducibility and verification as substantively important*. The scientific computing worldview takes seriously substantial methodological concerns about substantial reproducibility and substantial verification of substantial computational science, recognizing that substantial computational results without substantial methodological discipline are substantially less reliable than substantial alternatives. This commitment shapes practice substantially: with substantial reproducibility infrastructure, with substantial verification practices, with substantial appreciation that substantial scientific computing requires substantial methodological care that substantial general software development does not directly provide.

The fourth commitment is *to substantial humility about substantial computational results*. The scientific computing worldview takes seriously that substantial computational results have substantial uncertainty — from substantial input data, from substantial models, from substantial numerical methods — and that substantial competent practice substantially requires substantial humility about what substantial results actually establish. This commitment runs against engineering dispositions that treat computational outputs as definitive. Practitioners who have absorbed this commitment quantify substantial uncertainty seriously, communicate substantial uncertainty honestly, and recognize that substantial computational results are substantial estimates rather than substantial certainties.

The fifth commitment is *to substantial integration with substantial scientific domains*. The scientific computing worldview takes seriously that substantial scientific computing serves substantial scientific work, and that substantial competent practice substantially requires substantial engagement with substantial scientific domains. This commitment runs against engineering dispositions that prefer to remain within computer science. Practitioners who have absorbed this commitment engage substantially with substantial domain scientists, develop substantial substantive knowledge of substantial domains they work in, and treat substantial domain integration as integral to substantial scientific computing practice.

The sixth commitment is *to substantial accumulated scientific software as substantial infrastructure*. The scientific computing worldview takes seriously that substantial scientific software libraries (substantial LAPACK and substantial others, accumulated over substantial decades) constitute substantial scientific infrastructure that substantial science depends on, and that substantial maintaining and substantial extending this infrastructure is substantial scientific contribution rather than substantial purely technical work. This commitment shapes how substantial scientific software is treated and produced.

The seventh commitment is *to substantial cross-disciplinary engagement*. The scientific computing worldview takes substantial cross-disciplinary engagement seriously — with substantial mathematics, with substantial scientific disciplines, with substantial engineering disciplines that use substantial scientific computing. This commitment runs against engineering dispositions that prefer to remain within narrow disciplinary boundaries.

The eighth commitment is *to substantial open science and substantial open source*. The scientific computing worldview substantially aligns with substantial open science movements — substantial commitment to substantial reproducibility, substantial commitment to substantial sharing of substantial computational methods, substantial commitment to substantial open-source scientific software. The substantial overlap between substantial scientific computing and substantial open-source scientific software is substantial, with substantial portions of substantial canonical scientific software being substantial open source.

The ninth commitment is *to substantial honest engagement with substantial complexity of substantial scientific phenomena*. The scientific computing worldview takes seriously that substantial scientific phenomena are substantially complex, that substantial models necessarily abstract substantial reality, and that substantial computational results reflect substantial model assumptions rather than substantial unmediated reality. This commitment shapes practice with substantial humility about what substantial computational science establishes and substantial appreciation for substantial limits of substantial computational approaches.

These nine commitments — to numerical care, to mathematical foundations, to reproducibility and verification, to humility about results, to integration with scientific domains, to accumulated scientific software as infrastructure, to cross-disciplinary engagement, to open science, and to honest engagement with complexity — distinguish the scientific computing worldview from adjacent dispositions. Practitioners who have absorbed them work in scientific computing as substantial professional practice serving substantial scientific work.

### Common pitfalls in learning it

The pitfalls of learning scientific computing are characteristic and substantially distinctive — the substantial mathematical content, the substantial methodological concerns, and the substantial integration with substantial scientific domains produce many opportunities for misdirected learning.

The most common pitfall is *the math-avoidance trap*. Scientific computing has substantial mathematical content — substantial numerical analysis, substantial linear algebra, substantial differential equations, substantial probability and statistics — and learners can come to avoid the mathematics, relying on libraries to handle the mathematical content. The result is practitioners who can use scientific computing libraries for standard tasks but cannot reason about what they are doing, cannot adapt techniques to non-standard problems, cannot diagnose issues whose cause is mathematical. The countermeasure is to engage with the mathematics seriously: to study substantial numerical analysis, substantial linear algebra in computational contexts, the substantial various mathematical content scientific computing requires.

A second pitfall is *the library-as-discipline misconception*. Scientific computing is accessed substantially through libraries (NumPy, SciPy, LAPACK, the substantial various others), and learners can come to treat learning libraries as the substance of learning scientific computing. The result is practitioners who can use libraries for standard tasks but cannot reason about substantial underlying methods, cannot evaluate when libraries are appropriate, cannot adapt approaches when libraries do not suffice. The countermeasure is to engage with the substantial methods that libraries implement: to study what substantial algorithms libraries use, to understand their substantial numerical properties, to engage with substantial mathematical content rather than only with library calls.

A third pitfall is *the numerical-naivety trap*. Substantial naive numerical computation routinely produces substantial errors that practitioners do not notice — substantial floating-point issues that compound, substantial ill-conditioning that amplifies errors, substantial unstable algorithms that produce substantially unreliable results. Learners can come to write substantial numerical code without engaging with these concerns. The countermeasure is substantial engagement with substantial numerical analysis: substantial study of substantial numerical accuracy and stability, substantial practice with substantial documented numerical failures, substantial appreciation for what substantial reliable numerical computation actually requires.

A fourth pitfall is *the reproducibility-as-afterthought disposition*. Substantial reproducibility is substantively integral to substantial computational science, but learners can come to treat reproducibility as substantial concern to be addressed after substantial primary work is complete. The result is substantial computational work that lacks substantial reproducibility and that substantially fails to support substantial subsequent work. The countermeasure is substantial integration of reproducibility from substantial start: substantial use of substantial reproducibility tools (substantial containers, substantial workflow management, substantial various others) from substantial start of substantial work, substantial methodology that treats reproducibility as integral.

A fifth pitfall is *the domain-naive practice*. Substantial scientific computing serves substantial scientific work, and substantial competent practice substantially requires substantial domain engagement. Learners can come to develop substantial computational competence without substantial engagement with substantial scientific domains they work in, with substantial result that substantial computational work fails to substantially serve substantial scientific purposes. The countermeasure is substantial engagement with substantial scientific domains: substantial study of substantial domain content, substantial collaboration with substantial domain scientists, substantial appreciation for what substantial scientific work actually requires from substantial computation.

A sixth pitfall is *the publication-naive software development*. Substantial scientific software has substantial scientific publication concerns — substantial citation, substantial provenance, substantial reproducibility — that substantial general software development does not directly address. Learners can come to develop substantial scientific software without engaging with these substantial scientific concerns. The countermeasure is substantial engagement with substantial scientific software publication practices: substantial software citation standards, substantial software preservation, substantial reproducible research practices.

A seventh pitfall is *the performance-without-correctness focus*. Substantial scientific computing has substantial performance concerns, but substantial performance without substantial correctness is substantially worse than substantial slow but correct computation. Learners can come to focus on substantial performance optimization while neglecting substantial correctness concerns. The countermeasure is substantial appreciation for substantial correctness as substantial primary concern: substantial verification of substantial implementations against substantial expected results, substantial unit testing of substantial numerical code, substantial validation against substantial established results.

An eighth pitfall is *the uncertainty-blindness problem*. Substantial computational results have substantial uncertainty that substantial naive computation does not represent. Learners can come to produce substantial computational outputs without substantial uncertainty quantification, with substantial result that substantial outputs are substantially over-interpreted. The countermeasure is substantial engagement with substantial uncertainty quantification: substantial study of substantial methods for uncertainty quantification, substantial integration of uncertainty quantification into substantial computational work, substantial appreciation for what substantial uncertainty in substantial computational results means.

A ninth pitfall is *the LLM-shortcut problem* as applied to scientific computing. Modern AI tools can produce substantial scientific computing code, can answer substantial scientific computing questions. The cost is substantial in scientific computing because substantial competent practice depends on substantial mathematical understanding that develops through substantial direct engagement, on substantial appreciation for substantial numerical subtleties that develops through substantial practice with substantial numerical work. The countermeasure is to use AI tools as supplements to substantial direct engagement rather than as replacements.

A tenth pitfall is *the substantial machine-learning-everything trap* applied to scientific computing. Substantial recent enthusiasm for substantial machine learning in substantial scientific computing can produce substantial expectation that substantial machine learning will solve substantially all scientific computing problems, with substantial result that substantial classical numerical methods are substantially neglected when they would substantially better serve specific applications. The substantial classical scientific computing methods substantially work well for substantial problem categories. The countermeasure is substantial appreciation for substantial appropriate use of substantial different approaches: substantial classical methods for substantial problems where they work well, substantial machine learning where it substantially provides unique value, substantial integration when integration substantially benefits.

An eleventh pitfall is *the legacy-code-avoidance trap*. Substantial scientific computing involves substantial legacy code — substantial scientific software that has accumulated over substantial decades, substantial Fortran code that remains substantively important in substantial application areas. Learners can come to avoid substantial legacy code, preferring substantial newer technologies, with substantial result that they cannot engage with substantial scientific computing infrastructure that substantial science depends on. The countermeasure is substantial engagement with substantial legacy scientific software: substantial appreciation for what substantial accumulated scientific software provides, substantial engagement with substantial Fortran when relevant, substantial recognition that substantial scientific computing has substantial historical character that substantial competent practice engages with.

### The literature of the subject

The literature on scientific computing has substantial diverse character. The discipline has substantial canonical texts that remain substantively valuable across decades, substantial contemporary literature addressing substantial recent developments particularly in scientific machine learning, and substantial diversity across substantial application domains. Engagement requires substantial fluency with this diverse literature.

#### Canonical entry points

The substantial canonical introduction to scientific computing as integrated discipline is Heath's *Scientific Computing: An Introductory Survey* (third edition, 2018, available freely in classroom edition). The book substantially covers substantial breadth of scientific computing with substantial mathematical care and substantial accessibility, providing substantial preparation for substantial subsequent specialized study.

For numerical analysis specifically, Trefethen and Bau's *Numerical Linear Algebra* (1997) is unusually substantively well-written and remains the substantial canonical text for numerical linear algebra. Higham's *Accuracy and Stability of Numerical Algorithms* (second edition, 2002) is the substantial reference for substantial numerical analysis with substantial emphasis on substantial accuracy and stability concerns. Burden, Faires, and Burden's *Numerical Analysis* (tenth edition, 2015) provides substantial broader numerical analysis introduction.

For scientific computing in specific languages, the substantial various Python scientific computing books (the substantial *Python for Data Analysis* by McKinney, the substantial *Numerical Python* by Johansson) are substantial, though they substantially overlap with general Python material. The substantial *Numerical Recipes* in various editions and various languages is substantial historical reference, though substantial portions are substantially dated for contemporary practice.

For high-performance scientific computing, the substantial intersection with the HPC material in Section 4.8 is substantial. Pacheco and Malensek's *An Introduction to Parallel Programming* covers substantial parallel computing foundations.

For partial differential equations specifically, LeVeque's *Finite Difference Methods for Ordinary and Partial Differential Equations* is substantial. Strang and Fix's *An Analysis of the Finite Element Method* (second edition, 2008) is foundational for finite element work.

For Monte Carlo and stochastic methods, Robert and Casella's *Monte Carlo Statistical Methods* (second edition, 2004) is substantial. Liu's *Monte Carlo Strategies in Scientific Computing* covers similar material.

For scientific machine learning specifically, the substantial recent literature is rapidly developing. The substantial Karniadakis et al. work on physics-informed neural networks (the substantial various papers since 2017) constitutes substantial foundational literature. The substantial Brunton and Kutz *Data-Driven Science and Engineering* covers substantial broader data-driven scientific computing.

For learners interested in computational science specifically, Landau, Páez, and Bordeianu's *Computational Physics: Problem Solving with Python* covers substantial computational physics foundations. The substantial various computational chemistry, computational biology, the substantial various other domain-specific texts cover substantial domain-specific applications.

The reading-order recommendation: Heath as foundational text for substantial breadth, Trefethen and Bau for substantial numerical linear algebra depth, Higham for substantial numerical analysis methodology, with substantial domain-specific references for substantial specific scientific domains of interest. The substantial accompanying course materials from major universities (substantial MIT 18.330/18.335, substantial Stanford CS137, the substantial various others) provide substantial pedagogical complement.

The substantial various Coursera, edX, and substantial other online platforms have substantial scientific computing courses with substantial structured curriculum. The substantial Software Carpentry and substantial Data Carpentry materials provide substantial accessible introduction to substantial scientific computing practices.

#### The progression

After the canonical introduction, the progression in scientific computing branches according to specialization.

*Numerical analysis at depth.* For learners pursuing numerical analysis seriously, beyond Trefethen-Bau and Higham, Demmel's *Applied Numerical Linear Algebra* covers substantial numerical linear algebra with substantial implementation focus. Golub and Van Loan's *Matrix Computations* (fourth edition, 2013) is the substantial substantial reference for substantial matrix computations. The substantial papers in substantial numerical analysis journals (SIAM Journal on Numerical Analysis, the substantial various others) cover substantial active research.

*Differential equations at depth.* For learners pursuing differential equations specifically, Hairer, Nørsett, and Wanner's *Solving Ordinary Differential Equations* (two volumes, various editions) is substantial. Iserles's *A First Course in the Numerical Analysis of Differential Equations* covers similar material more accessibly. For PDEs, beyond LeVeque, the substantial *Spectral Methods in MATLAB* by Trefethen covers substantial spectral methods. The substantial various PDE books for finite elements, finite volumes, and the various other approaches extend substantial coverage.

*Optimization at depth.* For learners pursuing numerical optimization, Nocedal and Wright's *Numerical Optimization* (second edition, 2006) is substantial. Boyd and Vandenberghe's *Convex Optimization* (free) covers substantial convex optimization with substantial care.

*Statistical computing at depth.* For statistical computing, Robert and Casella cover substantial Monte Carlo methods. Brooks, Gelman, Jones, and Meng's *Handbook of Markov Chain Monte Carlo* covers substantial MCMC. Gelman et al.'s *Bayesian Data Analysis* (third edition, 2013) covers substantial Bayesian computation.

*High-performance scientific computing at depth.* Beyond the HPC material in Section 4.8, the substantial scientific-computing-specific HPC literature includes substantial work on substantial parallel scientific algorithms, substantial substantial GPU computing for scientific applications, substantial work on substantial heterogeneous computing for scientific work. The substantial PETSc, substantial Trilinos, substantial various other parallel scientific computing library documentation provide substantial reference.

*Scientific machine learning at depth.* The substantial recent literature on substantial scientific machine learning is rapidly developing. The substantial Raissi, Perdikaris, Karniadakis papers on physics-informed neural networks and substantial subsequent work constitute substantial foundational literature. The substantial various papers on substantial neural surrogate models, on substantial machine learning for substantial scientific discovery, on substantial integration of substantial machine learning with substantial classical scientific computing constitute substantial active research. Brunton and Kutz cover substantial broader data-driven approaches. The substantial various papers on substantial AlphaFold and substantial similar substantial scientific machine learning successes constitute substantial recent literature.

*Computational physics at depth.* Beyond Landau et al., Thijssen's *Computational Physics* covers substantial computational physics foundations. The substantial various computational physics texts and substantial papers on substantial various physics simulation domains extend the picture.

*Computational chemistry at depth.* Jensen's *Introduction to Computational Chemistry* (third edition, 2017) is substantial. Cramer's *Essentials of Computational Chemistry* covers similar material. The substantial various papers on substantial molecular dynamics, on substantial quantum chemistry computation, on substantial various computational chemistry methods extend the picture.

*Computational biology at depth.* The substantial computational biology literature is substantial. Durbin, Eddy, Krogh, and Mitchison's *Biological Sequence Analysis* is substantial for sequence work. The substantial various papers on substantial bioinformatics, substantial systems biology computation, substantial structural biology computation, the substantial various subareas extend the picture.

*Climate and earth system modeling at depth.* The substantial climate modeling literature is substantial. The substantial various climate science textbooks cover substantial climate science foundations that climate computing serves.

*Computational fluid dynamics at depth.* Versteeg and Malalasekera's *An Introduction to Computational Fluid Dynamics: The Finite Volume Method* (second edition, 2007) is substantial. The substantial various CFD texts and substantial papers on substantial CFD methods and applications extend the picture.

*Reproducible scientific computing.* The substantial reproducible scientific computing literature is substantial. Wilson et al.'s "Best Practices for Scientific Computing" and "Good Enough Practices" papers are substantial. The substantial various papers on substantial workflow management (Snakemake, Nextflow, the substantial various others), on substantial containerization for scientific computing, on substantial various reproducibility infrastructure constitute substantial substantial literature.

*Scientific software engineering.* The substantial scientific software engineering literature is substantial. Wilson's substantial work, the substantial various papers from the Better Scientific Software (BSSw) initiative, the substantial various papers on substantial scientific software practices constitute substantial literature.

*Uncertainty quantification at depth.* Smith's *Uncertainty Quantification: Theory, Implementation, and Applications* covers substantial UQ. The substantial various papers on substantial UQ methods, on substantial polynomial chaos expansions, on the substantial various UQ approaches extend the picture.

*Multiscale and multiphysics modeling.* The substantial multiscale modeling literature includes substantial papers on substantial bridging substantial scales in substantial scientific simulation, on substantial coupling substantial different physical models, the substantial various multiscale concerns.

#### Heterodox correctives

Several positions are heterodox correctives within scientific computing and worth engagement for the perspective they provide.

*The substantial reproducibility-skeptical perspective.* Substantial work documents that substantial computational science has substantial reproducibility problems substantially worse than substantial casually assumed. The substantial various papers on substantial computational reproducibility crises, on substantial documented reproducibility failures across substantial scientific domains, on substantial systemic concerns about substantial computational science constitute substantial heterodox literature with substantial intellectual content.

*The classical-numerical-methods perspective on substantial machine learning enthusiasm.* Substantial practitioners argue that substantial recent enthusiasm for machine learning in scientific computing has substantially overshadowed substantial classical numerical methods that substantially work better for substantial application categories. The substantial argument has substantial proponents and substantial intellectual content. Engagement with this perspective gives substantial perspective on what substantial appropriate use of substantial different approaches looks like.

*The substantial Fortran-defense perspective.* Substantial scientific computing depends substantially on substantial Fortran code that has accumulated over substantial decades, and substantial movement toward substantial newer languages has substantial implications for substantial scientific computing infrastructure. The substantial Fortran community produces substantial defenses of substantial Fortran's continued substantial value in substantial scientific computing, with substantial intellectual content.

*The "scientific software is undervalued" perspective.* Substantial work argues that substantial scientific software is substantially undervalued in substantial academic incentive structures relative to substantial scientific papers, with substantial implications for substantial scientific software quality and substantial scientific software sustainability. The substantial various papers on this concern from substantial scientific software engineering community constitute substantial heterodox literature.

*The substantial open science perspective.* Substantial work argues for substantial broader open science practices including substantial open data, substantial open source software, substantial open access publication, with substantial implications for how substantial scientific computing should be practiced. The substantial various open science writings constitute substantial heterodox literature.

*The substantial AI-skeptical perspective on AI for science.* Substantial work questions whether substantial AI applications to substantial science substantially produce substantial scientific value or substantially produce substantial appearance of substantial scientific value while substantially failing substantial scientific standards. The substantial various critical writings on substantial AI for substantial science constitute substantial heterodox literature.

*The substantial domain-expert-centered perspective.* Substantial work argues that substantial scientific computing should be substantially driven by substantial domain experts rather than by substantial computational specialists who lack substantial domain expertise, with substantial implications for substantial collaboration patterns and substantial training. The substantial argument has substantial proponents.

#### What to skip and why

Books on scientific computing published before approximately 2010 should be approached as substantial historical references rather than as guides to contemporary practice, with substantial exceptions of foundational books (Trefethen-Bau, Higham, Golub-Van Loan, the substantial various others that retain substantial value).

Numerical Recipes in its various editions should be approached with substantial caution. The book has substantial historical value but substantial portions are substantially dated, and substantial concerns about substantial license restrictions and substantial substantive concerns about substantial method choices have produced substantial criticism from substantial portions of the substantial scientific computing community.

Books on scientific computing that abstract over substantial mathematics should be approached with substantial caution. Substantial scientific computing substantially requires substantial mathematical engagement.

Books on substantial old scientific computing platforms or substantial old scientific computing approaches that have been substantially superseded should be approached as historical references.

Most marketing-driven books on specific scientific computing tools should be approached as substantial practical references rather than as substantial foundational learning.

Most online tutorials on scientific computing topics are uneven in quality. The exceptions — the substantial Software Carpentry and Data Carpentry materials, the substantial well-maintained scientific computing course materials, the substantial talks from substantial scientific computing conferences — are valuable.

Books that promise to teach scientific computing "without programming" or substantially abstracting over substantial computational implementation should be approached with substantial skepticism. Substantial scientific computing substantially requires substantial programming and substantial computational engagement.

Pre-machine-learning-integration books on scientific computing should be supplemented with substantial recent material covering scientific machine learning developments.

### A note on contemporary relevance

Scientific computing is among the more contemporarily active subjects on the application axis, with substantial developments substantially reshaping the discipline.

The most consequential contemporary development is the substantial integration of substantial machine learning with substantial scientific computing. The substantial work on substantial physics-informed neural networks, substantial neural surrogate models, substantial machine learning for substantial scientific discovery has substantially transformed substantial portions of substantial scientific computing practice. Substantial successes (substantial AlphaFold for substantial protein structure prediction, substantial various other substantial successes) have substantially demonstrated substantial value of substantial integration. The trajectory suggests continued substantial machine-learning-scientific-computing integration with substantial implications for substantial portions of practice.

The second major development is the substantial maturation of substantial reproducibility infrastructure. Substantial workflow management systems (Snakemake, Nextflow, the substantial various others), substantial containerization for substantial scientific computing, substantial various reproducibility tools have substantially advanced. The trajectory suggests continued substantial reproducibility infrastructure development.

The third development is the substantial growth of substantial AI for substantial science as substantial substantive movement. Substantial commitment from substantial major AI labs (substantial DeepMind, substantial various others) to substantial AI applications in substantial science has substantially elevated substantial AI-for-science work. The trajectory suggests continued substantial AI-for-science development with substantial uncertainty about substantial which directions will substantially produce substantial scientific value.

The fourth development is the substantial maturation of substantial GPU computing for substantial scientific applications. Substantial CUDA, substantial various other GPU computing frameworks, substantial various GPU-accelerated scientific computing libraries have substantially advanced. The trajectory suggests continued substantial GPU adoption for substantial scientific computing.

The fifth development is the substantial growth of substantial computational science as substantial recognized scientific methodology alongside substantial theory and substantial experiment. Substantial recognition of substantial computational science as substantial third pillar of substantial science has substantially advanced, with substantial implications for substantial scientific training, substantial scientific publication, substantial scientific funding.

The sixth development is the substantial reckoning with substantial scientific software sustainability concerns. The substantial recognition that substantial scientific software requires substantial maintenance and that substantial existing incentive structures substantially fail substantial scientific software has produced substantial response, including substantial various initiatives for substantial sustainable scientific software.

The seventh development is the substantial maturation of substantial cloud computing for substantial scientific applications. Substantial scientific cloud computing has substantially advanced, with substantial implications for substantial accessibility of substantial scientific computing resources beyond substantial supercomputing centers.

The eighth development is the substantial growth of substantial open science practices. Substantial requirements for substantial data sharing, substantial code sharing, substantial reproducible research from substantial funders and substantial publishers have substantially advanced. The trajectory suggests continued substantial open science development.

The ninth development is the substantial maturation of substantial uncertainty quantification as substantial mainstream concern. Substantial integration of substantial UQ into substantial scientific computing practice has substantially advanced, with substantial implications for what substantial competent computational science requires.

Despite these developments, the foundational character of scientific computing is substantially robust. The core concerns — numerical accuracy and stability, computation at scale for science, reproducibility and verification, uncertainty quantification, integration with scientific practice — remain as articulated decades ago. The contemporary developments add to this foundation rather than replacing it.

A final observation specific to scientific computing: among the application chapters, this is the one with the most substantial integration with substantial established disciplines outside computer science. Substantial competent practice substantially requires substantial engagement with substantial mathematics and substantial scientific disciplines. # Chapter 7 — The Mathematical Substrate

The mathematical substrate chapter occupies a position in this map distinct from the preceding axis and application chapters. Where Chapters 3 through 6 covered subjects whose intellectual content is substantially computer science proper, this chapter covers the substantial mathematical content that substantial portions of substantial computer science depend on. The mathematics treated here is not itself computer science — it is mathematics. But substantial portions of computer science substantially require this mathematics, and substantial competent practice in substantial CS subjects substantially depends on substantial mathematical preparation that is often substantially underrepresented in substantial CS curricula.

The chapter therefore has different character from the preceding chapters. It covers substantial mathematical subjects (linear algebra, calculus, probability theory, discrete mathematics, mathematical logic, abstract algebra, optimization, the substantial various others) with substantial focus on what these subjects provide for substantial CS work rather than on substantial mathematical concerns in their own right. Substantial mathematical subjects have substantial intellectual content that substantially extends beyond their CS applications, and learners pursuing substantial mathematical depth should engage with substantial mathematical literature in its own right rather than only through CS-applied lenses. But substantial portions of substantial CS subjects substantially require substantial mathematical preparation that this chapter substantially aims at making explicit and accessible.

A note on the relationship between substantial CS curricula and substantial mathematical preparation. Substantial portions of substantial contemporary CS programs substantially underemphasize substantial mathematical preparation relative to what substantial CS subjects substantially require for substantial competent practice. The substantial gap between substantial mathematical preparation that substantial CS programs typically provide and substantial mathematical preparation that substantial CS subjects substantially require is substantial, and substantial portions of substantial practitioner difficulty with substantial CS subjects can be substantially traced to substantial inadequate mathematical preparation that substantial CS curricula did not address. This chapter substantially aims at making this gap visible and at supporting practitioners in addressing it.

A further note on the relationship between substantial mathematics and substantial CS subjects. The relationship is asymmetric — substantial portions of substantial CS substantially depend on substantial mathematics, but substantial mathematics does not similarly depend on substantial CS. Substantial portions of substantial mathematics are substantially developed without substantial reference to substantial computational concerns, and substantial mathematicians often substantially regard substantial CS as substantial application area rather than as substantial mathematical subject in its own right. The substantial CS practitioner approaches substantial mathematics differently from substantial pure mathematician — with substantial focus on substantial mathematical content that substantially serves substantial CS work, with substantial less concern for substantial mathematical content that substantial CS does not directly use, with substantial different methodological approach that substantial computational engagement substantially affords. This chapter approaches substantial mathematics from substantial CS practitioner perspective rather than from substantial pure mathematician perspective.

A final note on the chapter's structure. The chapter does not aim at substantial comprehensive coverage of substantial mathematical subjects — substantial mathematical literature substantially exceeds what any single chapter can substantially cover. The chapter aims at substantial orientation: at making substantial mathematical preparation that substantial CS work substantially requires visible, at pointing to substantial appropriate references for substantial deeper engagement, at supporting practitioners in substantial mathematical development that substantial CS work substantially benefits from. Substantial deep mathematical engagement substantially requires substantial engagement with substantial mathematical literature in its own right rather than only through this chapter.

The chapter is organized by substantial mathematical subject, with substantial each section covering substantial what the subject is, substantial what CS subjects depend on it substantially, substantial what substantial preparation provides substantial CS practitioners, substantial how substantial study should proceed, and substantial what substantial reference material is appropriate. The order roughly tracks substantial dependency — substantial earlier subjects are substantially prerequisite for substantial later subjects in some cases, though substantial cross-references substantially complicate any strict ordering.

A reader pursuing substantial mathematical preparation as supplement to substantial CS work typically focuses on substantial subjects most relevant to substantial specific CS subjects of interest rather than on substantial comprehensive coverage of substantial mathematics. The chapter is written to support both substantial focused study (engagement with substantial specific mathematical subjects most relevant to substantial specific CS interests) and substantial broader survey reading (substantial overview of substantial mathematical preparation that substantial CS practice substantially benefits from).

A final note: this chapter substantially differs from preceding chapters in substantial important way. The preceding chapters covered subjects where substantial author has substantial direct expertise — the various CS subjects covered substantially. This chapter covers substantial mathematics where substantial author's expertise is substantial CS-applied rather than substantial pure mathematical. Substantial mathematical readers will find substantial treatment substantially different from substantial pure mathematical treatments, which is substantially intentional — the chapter aims at substantial CS practitioner needs rather than at substantial mathematical comprehensiveness. Substantial pure mathematicians may substantially appropriately find substantial treatment substantially incomplete from substantial pure mathematical perspective, and substantial recommendation is substantial engagement with substantial pure mathematical literature for substantial deeper mathematical understanding.

## 7.1 — Linear Algebra

### What it is, properly

Linear algebra is the substantial mathematical subject concerned with substantial vector spaces, substantial linear transformations between them, and substantial various structures that arise from these — substantial matrices, substantial determinants, substantial eigenvalues and eigenvectors, substantial inner product spaces, substantial various decompositions, the substantial broader theory that develops from substantial these foundational objects. Linear algebra is unusually substantial among mathematical subjects in CS preparation — substantial portions of substantial CS subjects substantially depend on substantial linear algebra, and substantial mathematical fluency in substantial linear algebra is among the most substantively transferable mathematical preparations for substantial CS work. The substantial centrality of substantial linear algebra to substantial portions of substantial computer science is substantially documented across substantial chapters of this map: substantial machine learning depends substantially on linear algebra throughout; substantial graphics depends substantially on linear algebra for substantial transformations and substantial various computations; substantial scientific computing depends substantially on numerical linear algebra; substantial data analysis depends substantially on linear algebraic methods; substantial signal processing depends substantially on linear algebra; substantial optimization depends substantially on linear algebra; the substantial various other CS subjects depend on linear algebra in substantial various ways.

Substantial CS-relevant linear algebra substantially emphasizes substantial computational aspects relative to substantial pure mathematical treatments, while substantial substantive understanding of substantial theoretical content substantially supports substantial computational work.
